```text
Queues the import requests for an integration run when all the Data Sources are in use.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Integration Run\[sn\_vul\_integration\_run\]

</td><td>

Vulnerability integration invocations.**Note:** If an integration run is in progress, only one integration can be in ready state in the backlog.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Integration Run Stats

 \[sn\_vul\_integration\_stats\]

</td><td>

Stores the breakdown of each step: for example, REST API time, VI creation time, and so on for an integration run

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Item Task\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_item\_task\]

</td><td>

Vulnerable items associated with problems, changes, and security incidents.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Malware Kit \[sn\_vul\_m2m\_malware\_kit\_vul\]

</td><td>

Contains the relationships between vulnerabilities and malware kits.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Prerequisite Solution\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_solution\_prerequisite\]

</td><td>

Contains the source-specific prerequisites to applying a solution, when available.

</td></tr><tr id="row_rate-limit"><td>

Vulnerability Rate limit\[sn\_vul\_rate\_limit\]

</td><td>

Defines a rate limit to be used on a scanner.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Reference\[sn\_vul\_reference\]

</td><td>

External references for known vulnerabilities.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Remediation Status\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_ttr\_status\]

</td><td>

Status of the vulnerable item against the closest applied remediation target rule.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Risk Rule\[sn\_vul\_calc\_risk\]

</td><td>

Specialized calculator rule used with the Risk Score calculators. Takes weights indicating which values, related to a VI, to use to calculate the Risk Score.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Rollup Calculator\[sn\_vul\_rollup\]

</td><td>

List of vulnerability rollup calculators.

</td></tr><tr id="row_scan"><td>

Vulnerability Scan\[sn\_vul\_scan\]

</td><td>

Vulnerability scan. Contains what to scan, with what scanner, and a summary of the scan results.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Scan Configuration Item\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_scan\_configuration\_item\]

</td><td>

Associates CMDB CIs that are queued to be scanned.

</td></tr><tr id="row_scan-queue"><td>

Vulnerability Scan Queue Entry\[sn\_vul\_scan\_q\_entry\]

</td><td>

Scan record queued for scanning or processing. Facilitates the requests within stated rate limits.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Scan Source\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_scan\_source\]

</td><td>

Associates sources to a scan record and signifies all the records that are queued to be scanned.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Scan Task\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_scan\_vulnerability\]

</td><td>

Associates vulnerability tasks for the sources of a scan record.

</td></tr><tr id="row_scanner"><td>

Vulnerability Scanner\[sn\_vul\_scanner\]

</td><td>

Defines third-party scanners to use in scans.

</td></tr><tr id="row_scanner-rate-limit"><td>

Vulnerability Scanner Rate Limit\[sn\_cmn\_scanner\_rate\_limit\]

</td><td>

Associates a scanner with a rate limit.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Software\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_entry\_software\]

</td><td>

Contains associations between vulnerabilities and vulnerable software.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Solution

 \[sn\_vul\_solution\]

</td><td>

Contains the relationship data between the vulnerability and the possible solutions for it. Starting with v24.0.6 of Vulnerability Response, a new column titled Scanner Bulletin is added to this table.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Solution

 \[sn\_vul\_m2m\_vulnerability\_solution\]

</td><td>

Links the Vulnerability Entry \(sn\_vul\_entry\) table to the Vulnerability Solution \(sn\_vul\_solution\) table.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability State Change Approval\[sn\_vul\_change\_approval\]

</td><td>

Tracks the approval process for vulnerabilities.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Superseding Solution\[sn\_vul\_m2m\_solution\_supersedence\]

</td><td>

Contains the source-specific relationship between solutions.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Update Manifest\[sn\_vul\_vuln\_update\]

</td><td>

Contains a list of vulnerabilities that need their rollup data updated after their vulnerable items are updated, closed, or have risk score changes.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Vendor Mapping

 \[sun\_vul\_vulnerability\_vendor\_mapping\]

</td><td>

Contains a list of vulnerabilities and their vendors.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerable Item\[sn\_vul\_vulnerable\_item\]

</td><td>

Contains the occurrence of a vulnerability on a configuration item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerable Item Detection

 \[sn\_vul\_detection\]

</td><td>

Contains the vulnerable item detections from third-party integrations.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerable Software\[sn\_vul\_software\]

</td><td>

Software that is known to have certain vulnerabilities.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>
```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\integration-with-grc.md

```text
---
title: Request an exception using GRC: Policy and Compliance Management
description: Request policy exceptions using the GRC policy exception management capability in the Policy and Compliance Management application from within Vulnerability Response.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Requesting and approving an exception, Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Request an exception using GRC: Policy and Compliance Management

Request policy exceptions using the GRC policy exception management capability in the Policy and Compliance Management application from within Vulnerability Response.

## Before you begin

Before you can use the Policy Exception Integration to request policy exceptions, you must download the GRC: Policy and Compliance Management application from the ServiceNow Store.

Role required: sn\_vul.remediation\_owner

You can also request exceptions in the classic UI:

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Vulnerable Items** \(or **Remediation Tasks**\)&gt;**All**, and open the item or group for which you want to request an exception.

    The selected item or group must be in Open, Under investigation, or Awaiting implementation state.

2.  On the selected form, click **Request Exception**.

3.  On the form, fill in the fields.

<table id="table_stm_xty_flb"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Policy

</td><td>

Vulnerability Management policy that you are requesting an exception for.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Control objective

</td><td>

Control objectives that are associated with the policy you selected. If a policy is not selected, all the control objectives are listed.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Valid from

</td><td>

Date when the exception will start. The default value is the current date. This date cannot be in the past.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Valid until

</td><td>

Date that the policy exception expires and the state of the vulnerable item or group changes from Deferred to Open.**Note:** The number of days that the policy exception is valid cannot exceed the **Maximum exception duration \(days\)** that you set for the policy in Policy and Compliance. For more information, see [Create a policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/policy-and-compliance-management/t_DefineAPolicy.md).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Reason

</td><td>

Reason for requesting an exception.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Justification

</td><td>

Details that are related to the reason why this request is being made. This mandatory field must be filled in by the remediation owner.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>4.  Click **Submit**.

    For more information on the Policy Exception Integration and the hand-off between the remediation owner and the compliance manager, see [Policy and Compliance Management optional setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/policy-and-compliance-management/policy-compliance-optional-steps.md).



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\jira-configure.md

```text
---
title: Configure rules in the agile tool configuration
description: Jira issues are created on the Jira platform if the records created in the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task match the rules created in the agile tool configuration.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 4
breadcrumb: [Understanding the Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response, Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Configure rules in the agile tool configuration

Jira issues are created on the Jira platform if the records created in the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task match the rules created in the agile tool configuration.

## Before you begin

Roles required: sn\_vul.app\_read\_all, sn\_vul\_container.read\_all, sn\_vul\_agile.agile\_tool\_admin or sn\_vul\_jira.admin.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Agile Management for VR** &gt; **Configure Agile Tools**.

2.  To create a new configuration rule in the Agile Tool Configuration, select **New**.

3.  On the form, fill in the fields.

<table id="table_ghy_33p_ssb"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Name

</td><td>

Name of the Agile Tool Configuration.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Order

</td><td>

Order of the Agile Tool Configuration.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Agile Tool

</td><td>

The agile tool to create issues on the specific agile platform.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Entity Table

</td><td>

Table on which the Configuration rule is to be applied.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Condition

</td><td>

Filter condition on the selected entity table to create Issues on the Agile platform.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Auto Issue Creation

</td><td>

The configuration rule will be queried when any new record is created in the selected entity table, only if the check box is selected.**Note:** The user must use list action to create issues for existing entity records.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Project

</td><td>

The project in which the Jira Issue will be created.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Issue Type

</td><td>

The type of issue to be created in a particular project.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Assign To

</td><td>

The assignee to whom the issue should be assigned upon creation of the issue. To keep the created issues as unassigned, select “None”.

</td></tr><tr><td>

State Mapping

</td><td>

The existing state mappings from the Agile Tool State Mapping table or create a new state mapping between the issue status in the agile platform and the ServiceNow entity table states.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>    ![Agile tool configuration form-sample](../image/agile-tool-config-vr.png)By default, when the application is installed Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task are displayed in the Entity Table field on the Agile Tool Configuration form.

4.  To add the Application Vulnerable Item and Container Vulnerable Item in the Entity Table choice list navigate to **System Properties** &gt; **sn\_vul\_agile.tablesToShow** and add all in the Value field.

<table id="table_ov3_45w_dyb"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Choices

</td><td>

Comma-separated values for a choice list.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Value

</td><td>

Set the desired value for property. All property values are stored as strings. When retrieving properties via the gs.getProperty\(\) method, treat the results as strings. For example, a true\|false property returns 'true' or 'false' \(strings\), not the boolean equivalent.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>5.  Select the existing state mapping record or create a new state mapping in the Agile Tool State Mapping.

    |Field|Description|
    |-----|-----------|
    |Name|Insert the unique name of the State Mapping.|
    |Agile Tool|Select the agile tool.|
    |Entity Table|Select the source table on which state mapping will be created.|
    |Project|Select the project in which the state will be mapped.|
    |ServiceNow Entity State|Select the state that you want to map with agile tool status.|
    |Agile Tool Status|Select the state that you want to map from vulnerability item states.|

6.  Click on **Save and Fetch Projects** to fetch the projects in the Project field Configuration rule and validate the credentials of the Agile tool.

    **Tip:** You can set the System Property **projectsPageSize** to fetch a particular number of projects per page.

7.  Navigate to **System Properties** &gt; **sn\_vul\_jira.projectsPageSize** and add the number of projects to be fetched per page in the Value field for Jira Cloud.

8.  Select the Project, Issue Type and Assign to in the Configuration rule after the projects are fetched.

    Default field mapping will get populated in the embedded list of Agile Tool Field Mappings. By default, Summary and Description fields of Issues are mapped.

9.  To map other issue fields with the ServiceNow fields, click on the **Add Field Mapping**.

    ![Agile Tool Configuration-Field Mapping modal sample](../image/agile-vr-field-mapping.png)

    **Note:**

    -   You must map String and Choice type fields only.
    -   Make sure all the mandatory issue fields from the Agile tool are mapped properly with the entity field.
    |Field|Description|
    |-----|-----------|
    |Issue Field|Contains the issue fields of the currently selected project and issue type in the Configuration Rule.|
    |Entity Fields|Contains the fields of the entity table selected in the Configuration Rule.|
    |Use the Script|Select to use the script for mapping the issue fields with entity fields.|
    |Script|Script to map the Issue Field with Entity Fields.|

10. Click **Submit** in the glide modal to submit the field mapping record in the Agile Tool Field Mappings table.

    **Note:** If you do not map the mandatory fields, issues will not be created for that configuration rule.

    Mandatory fields are displayed on the top in the Agile Tool Configuration form.

    All the field mapping for a particular Configuration rule are displayed in the embedded list of the Agile Tool Field Mapping table in the Agile Tool Configuration table.

11. Right-click in the header to save your changes.

    The saved record is visible in the Agile Tool Configuration table. If any record is created in the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task matches any Configuration rule the issue will be created for the record according to the mapping in the Configuration rule.

12. Navigate to **System Properties** &gt; **sn\_vul\_jira.cloudBulkSize** and set the number of issues to be created per batch in the Value field.

    **Note:** Maximum number of issues that can be created per batch in Jira cloud is 50\).

    You can set the batch size for creating issues on Jira Server.

13. Navigate to **System Properties** &gt; **sn\_vul\_jira.onPremBulkIssue** and set the number of issues to be created per batch in the **Value** field.

    You can also set the error count in the Value field in the system property to process the records in the manifest table to create Jira issues up to a specific error count.

14. Navigate to **System Properties** &gt; **sn\_vul\_agile.ManifestErrorCount** and set the maximum number of times till which the manifest with error should be processed.



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\jira-install.md

```text
---
title: Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with Jira
description: Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Jira product properly integrates with Application Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1

---

# Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with Jira

Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Jira product properly integrates with Application Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.

## Before you begin

Role required: admin.

Complete the following setup checklist prior to installation. These setup tasks are required for a smooth installation and configuration.

**Note:** This process applies only to applications that are downloaded to production instances. If you're downloading applications to non-production or development instances, it's not necessary to get entitlements. Proceed to [Activate a ServiceNow Store application](../../../security-incident-response/task/activate-entitled-store-app.md).

<table id="table_rv4_tpl_mcb"><thead><tr><th>

Setup tasks

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Verify that the Vulnerability Response application is installed and activated.

</td><td>

-   Jira Integration for Vulnerability Response requires the Vulnerability Response application.
-   For the most current version of Jira Integration for Vulnerability Response, verify you have the most current version of Vulnerability Response installed. See [KB0856498 Vulnerability Response Compatibility Matrix and Release Schema Changes](https://support.servicenow.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0856498).
-   If the application is not installed and activated see, [Install Vulnerability Response](https://servicenow.com/docs/bundle/utah-security-management/page/product/vulnerability-response/task/install-and-configure-vr.html).


</td></tr><tr><td>

Verify that the Integration Hub plugin is installed.

</td><td>

-   Jira Spoke requires the Integration Hub.
-   See [KB1112383](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB1112383) for Integration hub installation.
-   For more information, see [Legal schedules-IntegrationHub overview](https://www.servicenow.com/content/dam/servicenow-assets/public/en-us/doc-type/legal/snc-addendum-integrationhub.pdf).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Verify that the latest version of [Jira Spoke](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/integrate-applications/integration-hub/jira-spoke-v3-0-2.md) is installed.

</td><td>

See [KB0792632](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0792632) for Jira Spoke installation.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Verify that the Vulnerability Response Integration with Agile Management is installed.

</td><td>

Vulnerability Response Integration with Atlassian Jira requires this integration.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>## Procedure

1.  Log in to the instance you want to install the Jira Vulnerability Response integration on.

2.  Navigate to the ServiceNow Store.

3.  In the ServiceNow Store, search for the Vulnerability Response integration with Jira application.

4.  Click the application tile.

    Detailed information about the application you are installing is displayed.

    **Note:** Consider reading the **Other Requirements** and **Dependencies** sections, as applicable.

5.  Click **Request App** and enter your Now Support login credentials.

6.  Click **Get**.

7.  Enter the **Instance Name** and **Reason for the Instance**, and click **Validate Instance**.

8.  Click **Request**.

    You will receive an email with detailed installation instructions.

9.  Navigate to **System Applications** &gt; **Applications**.

10. Locate the application, select it, and click **Install**.

    Your application is automatically installed on your instance.



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\jira-prerequisites.md

```text
---
title: Preparing for the Jira Vulnerability Response integration
description: A successful Jira Vulnerability integration requires planning and the execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Jira Vulnerability Response integration assumes that you are familiar with the Jira product and API.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: reference
last_updated: "2026-03-12"

---

# Preparing for the Jira Vulnerability Response integration

A successful Jira Vulnerability integration requires planning and the execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Jira Vulnerability Response integration assumes that you are familiar with the Jira product and API.

## Components installed with the Agile Integration for Vulnerability Response and Jira Integration for Vulnerability Response

Several types of components are installed with activation of the Agile Integration for Vulnerability Response and Jira Integration for Vulnerability Response plugin, including tables and user roles.

<table id="table_tqh_wpw_zht"><thead><tr><th>

Role Title \[name\]

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Agile Tool Admin \[sn\_vul\_agile.agile\_tool\_admin\]

</td><td>

Allows you to create configuration rule for Agile Management to create or update issues. Create rule configurations for Agile Management.

</td></tr><tr><td>

JIRA Admin \[sn\_vul\_jira.admin\]

</td><td>

Allows you to select Agile tool to create issues for selected entity table.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>|Table|Description|
|-----|-----------|
|Agile Tool Configuration \[sn\_vul\_agile\_tool\_rule\_config\]|Contains configuration rules to create issues on the Agile platform.|
|Agile Tool Field Mapping \[sn\_vul\_agile\_tool\_field\_mapping\]|Contains the field mapping of the Configuration Rule.|
|Agile Tool State Mapping \[sn\_vul\_agile\_tool\_state\_mapping\]|Contains the state mapping of the Jira issue status with the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task state.|
|Agile Tool Manifest \[sn\_vul\_agile\_tool\_manifest\]|Contains payload for Issue creation.|
|Agile Tool Data Resource \[sn\_vul\_agile\_tool\_data\_resource\]|Contains the information about the created Jira Issue.|
|JIRA Admin \[sn\_vul\_jira.admin\]|Allows you to select Agile tool to create issues for selected entity table.|


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\jira-vuln-integration.md

```text
---
title: Understanding the Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response
description: The Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response enables you to leverage different issue-tracking and agile management tools for tracking efforts of vulnerability remediation.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Understanding the Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response

The Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response enables you to leverage different issue-tracking and agile management tools for tracking efforts of vulnerability remediation.

## Request apps on the Store

Visit the [ServiceNow Store](https://store.servicenow.com/sn_appstore_store.do#!/store/home) to view all the available apps, and for information about submitting requests to the store. For cumulative release notes information for all released apps, see the [ServiceNow Store version history release notes](https://docs.servicenow.com/bundle/store-release-notes/page/release-notes/store/sn-store-release-notes.html).

## Overview

ServiceNow® Vulnerability Response is integrated with Atlassian Jira to create issues corresponding to vulnerabilities and synchronized the issue entity in Jira and the vulnerability item entity in Vulnerability Response bi-directionally to reflect the latest updates. Vulnerability Managers require vulnerability items present in ServiceNow Vulnerability Response to be created and assigned to remediation users \(developers in case of Application Vulnerability Response\) in their preferred system of record and issue management tool such as Atlassian Jira. Any progress performed on the vulnerabilities in the issue management tool should be updated in ServiceNow Vulnerability Response for visibility to Vulnerability Managers. Further, any updates made by Vulnerability Managers in Vulnerability Response for vulnerability items must also be passed on to the issue management tool for visibility to remediation users.

## Available versions

|Release version|Release Notes|
|---------------|-------------|
|Vulnerability Response|[Vulnerability Response Integration release notes](https://servicenow.com/docs/csh?topicname=secops-vuln-resp-integrations-rn&version=utah&pubname=utah-release-notes)|
|Vulnerability Response and Configuration Compliance for Container|[Container Vulnerability Response release notes](https://servicenow.com/docs/en-US/bundle/utah-release-notes/page/release-notes/security-operations/secops-container-vuln-resp-rn.html)|
|Jira Spoke|[Jira spoke release notes](https://servicenow.com/docs/en-US/bundle/store-release-notes/page/release-notes/store/platform-capabilities/store-integrationhub-rn-jira.html)|

## Jira Vulnerability integrations

To view the Jira Vulnerability integration, navigate to **All** &gt; **Agile Management for VR** &gt; **Configure Agile Tools** .

The following integrations are included in the base system. These integrations are not all active by default.

After the initial run, every day, scheduled jobs are chained to run the integrations automatically in order. You can also execute individual scheduled jobs manually. Scheduled jobs simplify the vulnerability remediation life cycle by keeping the instance synchronized with other vulnerability management systems.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\manual-ingestion-template.md

```text
---
title: Template for manual ingestion of vulnerabilities
description: The template provides columns that must be filled with vulnerability and configuration item \(CI\) data. After the template is populated and uploaded, the data is processed.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: reference

---

# Template for manual ingestion of vulnerabilities

The template provides columns that must be filled with vulnerability and configuration item \(CI\) data. After the template is populated and uploaded, the data is processed.

## Instructions for populating the template

To enter the details in the template, follow the instructions provided in the following table.

<table id="table_tmb_q3m_v5b"><thead><tr><th>

Column

</th><th>

Required/Optional?

</th><th>

String length \(max\)/ Valid values

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Asset ID

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

255

</td><td>

Universally unique identifier \(UUID\) or Asset ID. It can be the value generated by a scanner.

</td></tr><tr><td class="sub-head" colspan="4">

Enter information in at least one of the following five columns:

</td></tr><tr><td>

MAC Address

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

24

</td><td rowspan="5">

Host or asset information. The combination of all values provided must be unique for an asset.

</td></tr><tr><td>

FQDN

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

255

</td></tr><tr><td>

NETBIOS

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

255

</td></tr><tr><td>

IP Address

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

255

</td></tr><tr><td>

Hostname

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

255

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">

The remaining columns don't depend on each other.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability ID

</td><td>

Required

</td><td>

255

</td><td>

Represents a vulnerability in the National Vulnerability Database \(NVD\) or an ID returned from a third-party scanner.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Summary

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

512

</td><td>

Brief description of the vulnerability. It is added to the database only when the Vulnerability ID is unavailable.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

critical, high, medium, low, none

</td><td>

Severity of the vulnerability. It is added to the database only when the Vulnerability ID is unavailable. If the record is empty, then by default it is considered as critical.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Port

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

Not applicable

</td><td>

Port from which the vulnerability is detected. The value must be a valid integer. If there is an invalid input, the record is skipped.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Protocol

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

40

</td><td>

Type of network protocol used.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Proof

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

4000

</td><td>

Represents the location or system path of the vulnerability.

</td></tr><tr><td>

State

</td><td>

Optional

</td><td>

open, fixed

</td><td>

Status of the detection. If the record is empty, then by default it is considered open.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>
```

```text
# Perform a manual NVD integration import

If your initial import failed, or you don’t want to wait for the scheduled initial import, you can perform a full data import independent of the daily or weekly scheduled job.

## Before you begin

**Note:** The NIST National Vulnerability Database Integration- API \(CPE only\) and the NIST National Vulnerability Database Integration-API \(Unmapped CPE\) integrations are inactive by default. See [Activate the NIST National Vulnerability Database–API \(CPE only\)](install-nvd.md#) and [Activate the NIST National Vulnerability Database–API \(Unmapped CPE\)](install-nvd.md#) for more information.

Role required: sn\_vul.vulnerability\_admin

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response or Application Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Integrations**.

2.  Choose and integration, for example, the NIST National Vulnerability Database Integration - API \(CVE only\) integration.

3.  Select **Execute Now**.

    **Note:** If the NIST National Vulnerability Database Integration-API \(Unmapped CPE\) Integration is activated, associated CPE data is stored in the Vulnerable Software related list of the NVD vulnerability entry. See [Vulnerability Response vulnerability form fields](../../../vulnerability-response/reference/vulnerability-fields.md).

4.  For integration run statuses see, [View the \(National Vulnerability Database\) NVD integration import run status](nvd-check-imports.md).


**Parent Topic:**[Optional NVD integration modification and activities](../concept/nvd-optional-modifications.md)


```

```text
# Perform a manual Shodan exploit import

If your initial import failed, or you do not want to wait for the scheduled initial import, you can perform a full data import independent of the daily scheduled job.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.vulnerability\_admin or sn\_vul.admin \(deprecated\)

Persona and granular roles are available to help you manage what users and groups can see and do in the Vulnerability Response application. For an initial assignment of the persona roles in Setup Assistant, see [Assign the Vulnerability Response persona roles using Setup Assistant](../../../vulnerability-response/task/vr-assign-personas_SA.md). For more information about managing granular roles, see [Manage persona and granular roles for Vulnerability Response](../../../vulnerability-response/task/vr_manage_granular-roles.md).

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Shodan Exploit Integration** &gt; **Integrations**.

2.  Choose an integration.

3.  Select the **Full data import** check box.

4.  Click **Execute Now**.

    Once the import is complete, the check box is unchecked and delta imports resume.



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\manually-ingest-vulnerabilities.md

```text
---
title: Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities
description: Manually ingest vulnerabilities into the Vulnerability Response application so that you can remediate them quickly without having to wait for scanner results.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities

Manually ingest vulnerabilities into the Vulnerability Response application so that you can remediate them quickly without having to wait for scanner results.

Third-party scanners such as Rapid7 help import vulnerability data into Vulnerability Response and process the data to report the findings. With Manual Ingestion integration, you can proactively ingest the vulnerabilities and remediate them instead of waiting for scanners to report the assets that are at risk. Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities effectively protects the assets against unknown threats such as zero-day exploits.

## Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities

You can import the vulnerability data by uploading a template in the following file formats:

-   Excel
-   Comma-separated value \(CSV\)

To access and download the template, navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Manual Vulnerability Item Ingestion** &gt; **Upload File UI**.

Ensure that you follow these key points while populating the records in the template:

-   Do not make any typing errors in the Severity/ State column records. If there is a typing error, the record is skipped.
-   Do not change the column names.
-   Do not put column names in any row except the first row.
-   Do not change the order of the sheets in the Microsoft Excel template. The vulnerabilities data must always be present in the second sheet.
-   Do not put vulnerabilities details in any sheet except the Input Manual Detections sheet.
-   Do not use any character except alphanumeric and special characters such as dash \(-\), period \(.\), plus \(+\), underscore \( \_ \), space, brackets ‘\(‘ ‘\)’, and at symbol \(@\) for the filename.

For instructions on how to populate the data in the template, see [Template for manual ingestion of vulnerabilities](../reference/manual-ingestion-template.md).

Starting with v24.0.6 of Vulnerability Response, you can also create additional columns in the Microsoft Excel template. For more information, see [KB1646630](https://support.servicenow.com/nav_to.do?uri=%2Fkb%3Fid%3Dkb_article_view%26sys_kb_id%3D7fb3462b9348d2d4d744b94c5cba1001).

## Manual Ingestion integrations

The integration is triggered when a file is uploaded. Based on the type of file uploaded, the related integration is triggered.

To view the integrations, navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Manual Vulnerability Item Ingestion** &gt; **Integrations**.

The following base system Manual Ingestion integrations are available by default.

|Integration type|Description|
|----------------|-----------|
|Manual Ingestion Excel Integration|Retrieves data by fetching the latest Microsoft Excel file uploaded from the Vulnerability Ingestion Push Queue table and copies the attachment to the respective Integration run.|
|Manual Ingestion CSV Integration|Retrieves data by fetching the latest CSV file uploaded from the Vulnerability Ingestion Push Queue table and copies the attachment to the respective Integration run.|

The Manual Ingestion third-party integration creates an integration instance, by default, with the following two integration instance parameters:

-   max\_input\_records: Defines the number of records that can be created in the template. If you add more records than what is specified in this parameter, the additional records are skipped. The number of records can be updated in the Value column. The recommended number of record creations in the template is 1000. Anything above this value can pose performance challenges.
-   insert\_fixed: Imports fixed vulnerability detections.

To view the integration instance parameters, navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Manual Vulnerability Item Ingestion** &gt; **Integrations** and click the source instance of any integration. Alternatively, you can navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Integration Instances** and click **Manual Ingestion**.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\migrate-to-usem.md

```text
---
title: Migrate to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) from Vulnerability Response
description: Use the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool to migrate to the Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) platform for a smooth and secure transition.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Vulnerability Response implementation, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Migrate to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) from Vulnerability Response

Use the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool to migrate to the Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) platform for a smooth and secure transition.

## Before you begin

Role required: Admin

Ensure that you:

-   Run the migration in a non-production instance before attempting to use it in production.
-   Install the Unified Security Exposure Management application from the store.
-   Upgrade to USEM using the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool if you’re migrating from a version below 30.0.

**Note:** If you aren’t ready to migrate to USEM, select Upgrade later. The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management is hidden from view but remains accessible through the Filter Navigator.

## About this task

The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool provides a guided user interface and helps with preparation, conflict resolution, post-migration verification, and more. It verifies that all records and integrations are correctly updated.

The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool is included with the Unified Security Exposure Management application. Install the Unified Security Exposure Management application \[sn\_vul\_usem\_common\] before you install v30.x of any of the other Vulnerability Response applications.

The Unified Security Exposure Management application \[sn\_vul\_usem\_common\] is available in the ServiceNow® Store.

See the [Migration Guidance to Unified Security Exposure Management](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB2556844) Knowledge Base article for more details about the migration.

## Procedure

1.  **Accessing the** Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management tool: Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management**.

2.  **Preparation**: Review the customizations that may have to be resolved after upgrading to USEM.

3.  **Migration**:

    1.  Review the ordered list of applications to upgrade in the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management.
    2.  Upgrade applications in the order displayed \(core applications first, then integrated applications\).

        **Note:** Follow the upgrade order strictly to avoid dependency issues.

    3.  Confirm all applications display the status `Completed`.
    4.  Verify all post-upgrade scheduled jobs are completed \(status shows `upgrading job, running` until jobs are finished\).

        **Note:** You can’t proceed until all upgrades are complete.

4.  **Resolve conflicts**: Address any upgrade conflicts found.

5.  **Complete migration**:

    The post-migration step restores the turned off items and validates the results.

    1.  Navigate to the post-migration page of the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management.
    2.  Select **Enable all** to restore integrations and jobs.
    3.  Review the summary showing the number of integrations restored, scheduled jobs re-enabled, and migration rules successfully migrated.
6.  **Verify completion**:

    1.  Run sample Vulnerability Response jobs or check integration connectivity to verify instance functionality.
    2.  Review any unresolved upgrade conflicts.
    3.  Review the list of re-enabled integrations and jobs
    4.  Review the number of successfully migrated VR migration rules
    5.  Review any unresolved upgrade conflicts \(if applicable\)
    Your instance is ready to operate with USEM. You’re redirected to the lookup rule page.


## Result

The migration to Unified Security Exposure Management is complete. All integrations and jobs are reactivated, migration rules are successfully migrated, and the instance is ready for use.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\migrating-to-usem.md

```text
---
title: Migrating from Vulnerability Response to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\)
description: The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management is a centralized utility that guides and automates the migration from Vulnerability Response applications to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\). USEM provides a unified foundation that consolidates data models, streamlines features, and enhances performance and scalability across all Security Exposure Management applications.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Exploring the Vulnerability Response application, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Migrating from Vulnerability Response to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\)

The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management is a centralized utility that guides and automates the migration from Vulnerability Response applications to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\). USEM provides a unified foundation that consolidates data models, streamlines features, and enhances performance and scalability across all Security Exposure Management applications.

## Why Migrate to USEM

Migrating to USEM delivers significant benefits, including improved performance, simplified workflows, advanced analytics, unified exception management, and better scalability; empowering security teams to respond faster to emerging threats.

## How does the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management help

Migrating from Vulnerability Response products to USEM can be complex, involving changes to data models, plugins, integrations, and workflows. The Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management simplifies this process through:

-   **Guided experience**: A step-by-step interface ensures all critical actions and prerequisites are completed.
-   **Conflict resolution**: Ensures that prerequisites are met of and assists in upgrading other related apps to USEM.
-   **Post-migration verification**: Confirms that records and integrations are updated correctly and provides best practices for post-migration checks.

The tool is modular, reduces risk and manual effort, and automates data transfer, rule mapping, and configuration updates making your transition to USEM seamless and efficient.

-   **[Planning for migration from Vulnerability Response to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\)](usem-migration-planning.md)**  
When upgrading to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) v30.0, you can choose between two primary methods: the Migration assistant for Unified Security Exposure Management and the Store App Manager. This topic outlines the capabilities of each method across key migration steps.

**Parent Topic:**[Vulnerability Response](../reference/vuln-landing-page.md)

**Related topics**  


[Migrate to Unified Security Exposure Management \(USEM\) from Vulnerability Response](../task/migrate-to-usem.md)


```

```text
---
title: Request assignment group recommendations for a vulnerable item
description: Request Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations so that you can view assignment group recommendations. You can request recommendations if a matching assignment group is not available or is incorrect for a vulnerable item \(VI\).
locale: en-US

product: Vulnerability Response

topic_type: task

---

# Request assignment group recommendations for a vulnerable item

Request Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations so that you can view assignment group recommendations. You can request recommendations if a matching assignment group is not available or is incorrect for a vulnerable item \(VI\).

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.remediation\_owner

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Vulnerable Items** and open a VI.

    The item must be in the Open, Deferred, Under investigation, or Awaiting implementation state.

2.  Click the recommend icon \(![Recommend icon.](../image/Recommend_icon.png)\) next to the **Assignment group** field for assignment suggestions for that VI.

3.  From the Assignment Group Recommendations dialog box, select a recommendation that is based on the confidence score.

    The confidence score predicts how relevant the match is for the VI. For more information, see [Machine Learning solutions for Vulnerability Response](../concept/ml_vr.md).

4.  Click **Assign**.


## Result

The selected group appears in the **Assignment group** field and the Assignment type changes to Recommendation.


```

```text
# Configure Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for Vulnerability Response

Enable Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to reduce the time that you spend on identifying the owners for vulnerability findings that are unassigned or incorrectly assigned.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.manage\_assignment\_recom\_config

## About this task

This application is not enabled by default.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Assignment Recommendations**.

2.  Select **Enable Assignment Recommendations**.

    The **Predictive Intelligence solution definition field** appears. The default value is **Vulnerable Item Assignment**.

3.  Click **Save**.



```

```text
---

# Install Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for Vulnerability Response

Install Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations so that you can reduce the time that you spend on identifying owners for vulnerability findings that are unassigned or incorrectly assigned. This application is available as a separate subscription in the ServiceNow Store.

## Before you begin

Before you run the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations feature of Vulnerability Response in your ServiceNow AI Platform® instance, you must first download and install the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for Vulnerability Response application. Complete the following setup checklist prior to installation. These setup tasks are required for a smooth installation and configuration.

**Note:** This process applies only to applications that are downloaded to production instances. If you're downloading applications to non-production or development instances, it's not necessary to get entitlements. For more information, see [Activate a ServiceNow Store application](../../security-incident-response/task/activate-entitled-store-app.md).

Roles required: admin

<table id="table_rv4_tpl_mcb"><thead><tr><th>

Setup tasks

</th><th>

Action

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Verify that the Vulnerability Response application is installed and activated.

</td><td>

Navigate to **All** &gt; **System Applications** &gt; **All Applications** &gt; **Installed in your instance**. Vulnerability Response is shown in this list if it is installed.

 If the application is not installed and activated, see [Install Vulnerability Response](install-and-configure-vr.md).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Verify that the Predictive Intelligence application is installed and activated.

</td><td>

Navigate to **System Applications** &gt; **All Applications** &gt; **Installed in your instance**. Predictive Intelligence is shown in this list if it is installed.

 If the application is not installed and activated, see [Install Predictive Intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/install-predictive-intelligence.md).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Verify that you have the required ServiceNow roles for your instance.

</td><td>

Check that you have these required roles for installation and verification of expected results:-   vulnerability\_admin: Required to enable and configure Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations. You can also use it to view and select assignment recommendations for vulnerable items and remediation tasks.
-   ServiceNow admin: Required to install applications.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>## Procedure

1.  Navigate to the ServiceNow Store.

2.  Click **Log In** and enter your HI credentials.

3.  To view the available ServiceNow products, click the ServiceNow **Products** tab.![Install store ServiceNow page for ML.](../image/ml_install-store-SN-page.png)

4.  To view all the associated applications that you are entitling for Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations, click the Vulnerability Response product.

    The page lists all the applications that you are eligible for if you opt-in. For information on an application, click the link for the application.

5.  To verify that you have entitlement to Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations, click **Opt-in**.

6.  To agree to the terms and conditions, at the prompt, select the check box and click **Accept**.

    A message appears that indicates you have successfully opted-in for the application.

7.  Skip to step 10 to install the application on your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.

8.  If you want to manage your entitlement for the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application on other ServiceNow AI Platform instances, click **Manage Entitlement**.

    **Note:**

    If the **Manage Entitlement** button is not displayed, click the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application on the Product List to display it.

    1.  Choose an option.

    2.  Click **OK** or **Cancel** to continue.

    You are ready to activate plugins and install the application on your ServiceNow AI Platform instances.

9.  Log in to the ServiceNow AI Platform instance that you want to install the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application on.

10. Navigate to **All** &gt; **System Applications** &gt; **All Available Applications** &gt; **All**.

11. From the applications listed, locate the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application \(sn\_vul\_recom\) and click **Install**.

    The Application installation dialog box is displayed and any dependencies that are installed are displayed.

12. If you want demo data, select the **Load demo data** check box and click **Install**.

    **Note:** If you do not select the **Load demo data** check box, demo data is not available to install from the Application Manager later. For information on how to install or reinstall demo data after the initial installation, see the [Workaround to install demo data if application is already installed \[KB0722909\]](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0722909) article in the Now Support Knowledge Base.

    This installation may take some time. A message is displayed in the Install dialog box after the application and its dependencies are successfully installed.

13. Click **Close**.

14. To configure Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations, see [Configure Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for Vulnerability Response](ml-config-assignment-rec.md).



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\ml-request-bulk-edit-vg.md

```text
---
title: Request assignment group recommendations for a remediation task
description: Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view assignment group recommendations for a remediation task \(VUL\).
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Viewing assignment recommendations, Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Request assignment group recommendations for a remediation task

Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view assignment group recommendations for a remediation task \(VUL\).

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.remediation\_owner

## About this task

Use the `assignment_recommendation_batchsize` system property to set the batch size when you request Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for VULs and vulnerable items \(VIs\) using the bulk edit option. The default value of this system property is set for optimum prediction performance.

**Note:** If you increase the default value, the response time for Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for these cases might change.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Remediation Tasks** and select a VUL.

    The group must be in the Open, Under investigation, or Awaiting implementation state.

2.  Click the recommend icon \(![Recommend icon.](../image/Recommend_icon.png)\) next to the **Assignment group** field for the assignment group recommendations for the selected VUL.

    The Assignment Group Recommendations dialog box displays the recommended assignment groups with the average confidence score for all the VIs in that group.

3.  Select a recommendation from the Assignment group list based on the confidence score.

    The confidence score predicts how relevant the match is for the selected VUL.

4.  Click **Assign**.

    The selected group appears in the **Assignment group** field.

5.  Save the remediation task with the updated information.


## Result

All the VIs with the same assignment group as the VUL are assigned to the selected assignment group. The Assignment type changes to Recommendation for the VUL, as well as all the modified VIs.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\ml-request-bulk-edit-vis.md

```text
---
title: Request assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items
description: Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view the assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items \(VIs\) using a bulk edit. When multiple VIs are selected, a prediction is performed on a random sample of the selected VIs.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Viewing assignment recommendations, Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Request assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items

Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view the assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items \(VIs\) using a bulk edit. When multiple VIs are selected, a prediction is performed on a random sample of the selected VIs.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.remediation\_owner

## About this task

The recommendations and their confidence scores may differ slightly between invocations, based on the samples taken from the selected VIs. The confidence score evaluates whether the system is confident that the recommended assignment group is suited to resolve the vulnerability. Use the `assignment_recommendation_batchsize` system property to set the batch size when you request Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for remediation tasks \(VULs\) and VIs using the bulk edit option. The default value of this system property is set for optimum prediction performance.

**Note:** If you increase the default value, the response time for Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for these cases might change.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Vulnerable Item** and select multiple VIs.

    The items must be in the Open, Under investigation, or Awaiting implementation state.

2.  Click **Bulk Edit**.

3.  On the form, fill in the fields.

    |Field|Description|
    |-----|-----------|
    |Record selection|Records to be selected for bulk edit.|
    |Assignment group|Assignment group to set for the selected vulnerable items. To view the recommended assignment groups, click the recommend icon \(![Recommend icon.](../image/Recommend_icon.png)\) next to the field. Select a recommendation that is based on the confidence score and click **Assign**.|
    |Work notes|Notes to be added.|

4.  Click **OK**.


## Result

The selected group appears in the **Assignment group** field and the Assignment type changes to Recommendation for all the selected VIs.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\ml-train-sol-definition.md

```text
---
title: Create and train a solution definition for Vulnerability Response
description: Create and train a solution definition model for Vulnerability Response by using Predictive Intelligence. You can use this model to predict the assignment group for a vulnerable item \(VI\) or remediation task \(RT\) based on existing data.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Install Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations for Vulnerability Response, Additional Vulnerability Response setup and configuration tasks, Vulnerability Response implementation, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Create and train a solution definition for Vulnerability Response

Create and train a solution definition model for Vulnerability Response by using Predictive Intelligence. You can use this model to predict the assignment group for a vulnerable item \(VI\) or remediation task \(RT\) based on existing data.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul.vulnerability\_admin

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Predictive Intelligence** &gt; **Classification** &gt; **Solution Definitions**.

2.  On the Classification Definitions form, click **Vulnerable Item Assignment**.

    This is the default solution definition.

    **Note:**

    -   The default Training Frequency is 30 days. This means that every 30 days, the model retrains automatically using the VI dataset that matches the configured filter condition.
    -   Fields from the Vulnerable Item table can be used for training. The default solution definition trains on two VI fields: **Vulnerability.Summary** and **Configuration item.Name**. You can customize the fields used for training, depending on which fields you find helpful for predicting vulnerability ownership in your organization.
3.  To create and train the classification solution, see [Create and train a classification solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-solution-definition.md).

    **Note:** You can also create and train a similarity or a clustering solution. For more information, see:

    -   [Create and train a similarity solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-similarity-solution.md).
    -   [Create and train a clustering solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-clustering-solution.md).
4.  Click **Update &amp; Retrain**.

    **Note:** To configure advanced settings for ML solutions, see [Configuring advanced settings for your ML solutions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/configuring-advanced-settings-ml-solutions.md).



```

```text
# Viewing assignment recommendations

An assignment group is usually allocated to a vulnerable item by using assignment rules. These rules are configured by the vulnerability administrator to allocate the correct assignment groups that are based on the data received from third-party scanners.

For more information, see . If a matching assignment group is not available or is incorrect, you can use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view alternative assignment groups.

Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to do the following actions:

-   [Request assignment group recommendations for a vulnerable item](../task/ml-assignment-rec-for-vi.md).
-   [Request assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items](../task/ml-request-bulk-edit-vis.md).
-   [Request assignment group recommendations for a remediation task](../task/ml-request-bulk-edit-vg.md).


```

You can reduce the time that you spend on identifying the owners for unassigned or incorrectly assigned vulnerability findings. Also, you can see a system-generated confidence score that evaluates if the recommended assignment group is suited to resolve the vulnerability.

```text
---

# Machine Learning solutions for Vulnerability Response

Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations uses ServiceNow® Predictive Intelligence and machine learning to recommend assignment groups for vulnerable items \(VIs\) and remediation tasks \(VULs, or RTs\). You can reduce the time that you spend on identifying the owners for unassigned or incorrectly assigned vulnerability findings. Also, you can see a system-generated confidence score that evaluates if the recommended assignment group is suited to resolve the vulnerability.

The Predictive Intelligence capabilities are used in Vulnerability Response to provide better work experiences by creating and training a solution definition model that can predict the assignment group based on existing data.

For more information, see [Predictive Intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/predictive-intelligence.md).

## Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations

With the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application \(com.snc.vulnerability.recom\), the system can assign recommendations in Vulnerability Response using ServiceNow Predictive Intelligence. This plugin requires:

-   Vulnerability Response \(`com.snc.vulnerability`\)
-   Predictive Intelligence \(`com.glide.platform_ml`\)

For more information, see [Predictive Intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/predictive-intelligence.md).

## Confidence scores

Use the Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations application to generate the confidence score that reports the confidence level of the recommended assignment groups for the selected RTs or VIs. The confidence score evaluates whether the system is confident that the recommended assignment group is suited to resolve the vulnerability. Up to 10 recommendations appear. These recommendations are based on the highest probability for successful assignments. The first recommendation in the list means that the system has the highest confidence that it is the correct assignment group.

For example, on a scale of 0-100, for each assignment group, the confidence score suggests the probability of a VI or RT belonging to that particular group.

**Related topics**  


[Predictive Intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/predictive-intelligence.md)

[Create and train a classification solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-solution-definition.md)

[Create and train a similarity solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-similarity-solution.md)

[Create and train a clustering solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/create-clustering-solution.md)

[Configuring advanced settings for your ML solutions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/intelligent-experiences/predictive-intelligence/configuring-advanced-settings-ml-solutions.md)


```

```text
# Filter records with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app

Set additional filters to limit the number of records that are displayed on a screen. Filtering records in the mobile app works like filtering with a condition builder on the ServiceNow AI Platform.

## Before you begin

Role required: Remediation owner \(sn\_vul.remediation\_owner\)

## About this task

Time to complete this task: 5 minutes.

## Procedure

1.  To further refine your search results, or to enter additional filter criteria on any list of records that is displayed, tap the filter icon \(![Filter icon.](../../grc-common/image/grc-filter-image.png)\).

2.  With the Filters screen displayed, tap a field to expand it.

    A common field to enter search criteria for remediation task records is the State field.

3.  From the options that are displayed, tap the check box or tap an item for your filter.

    For many of the filters, you can select more than one option.

4.  Tap the Back icon \(![Back arrow icon.](../../security-incident-response/image/back.jpg)\) or **DONE** to save your changes to a field.

    The criteria you chose are displayed on the Filters screen. The number of records that match your criteria is also displayed at the top of the screen. Tap **RESET** to remove any filters and restore the default setting.

5.  Repeat steps 1 - 4 with the Filters screen displayed to continue setting your filter criteria.

6.  Tap the intersection icon \(![Intersection icon.](../image/mobile-filter-1.jpg)\) to create AND conditions.

7.  Alternatively, tap the intersection icon \(![Union icon.](../image/mobile-filter-2.jpg) \) to create OR conditions.

8.  After you have added all the criteria you want, with the Filters screen displayed, tap **DONE**.

    On the screen, only the records that match your filter criteria are displayed.


**Related topics**  


[Search for remediation tasks with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](mobile-vr-search-vgs.md)


```

```text
# View, assign, and edit remediation tasks with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app

View, assign, or edit the fields on remediation task \(RT\) records assigned to your group. Navigate through vulnerabilities, vulnerability items, and solutions records and related lists to view solutions, deployment metrics, and items to address.

## Before you begin

Role required: Remediation owner \(sn\_vul.remediation\_owner\)

## About this task

Assign RT \(VUL\) records to yourself or reassign them to another member of your group. Alternatively, view the details of active remediation task records, Vulnerable Items \(VI\), NVD vulnerabilities, and solutions prior to editing, assignment or remediation. Time to complete this task: 5-10 minutes.

## Procedure

1.  If you are not logged in to your ServiceNow AI Platform instance on your mobile device, for more information see [Log in to the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](mobile-vr-login-mobileapp.md).

2.  With the Vulnerability Response landing screen displayed, tap **Assigned to My Groups**.

    If you navigate away from the Vulnerability Response Mobile app after you have logged in, tap the Now Agent app at any time to return to the last screen you had displayed.

    ![Assigned to My Groups highlighted.](../image/mobile_vr_app_redo.jpg)

    The list of open records assigned to your group is displayed.

    ![Assigned to my groups list.](../image/mobile-vr-vg-list.jpg)

3.  Refer to [Search for remediation tasks with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](mobile-vr-search-vgs.md) to search for records that match specific criteria.

4.  Alternatively, with the filter icon \(![Filter icon.](../image/mobile-vr-filter-image.png)\) displayed, [Set filters to limit the number of records](mobile-filter-records.md) on the list.

    Filtering records on screens in the mobile app works like filtering with a condition builder on the ServiceNow AI Platform.

5.  To reassign or edit a record directly from the list, with the list of open records displayed, swipe left on a record to open the menu.

    ![Assigned to My Groups swipe menu.](../image/mobile-vr-vg-swipe-vg.jpg)

6.  Choose one from the menu that is displayed to continue.

<table id="choicetable_a1k_fvg_yhb"><thead><tr><th align="left" id="d445115e171">

Option

</th><th align="left" id="d445115e174">

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td id="d445115e180">

**Assign to me**

</td><td>

If the RT record is already assigned to you, this option is not displayed.Tap **Assign to Me** to assign the RT to yourself. Your name is displayed in the Assigned to field on the record.

 A message is displayed that confirms the assignment.

</td></tr><tr><td id="d445115e198">

**Reassign**

</td><td>

To reassign the RT to an assignment group:1.  Tap **Assignment group**.
2.  From the list, tap a group or enter text in the search field.
 To assign or reassign the RT to a member of an assignment group:

 1.  Tap **Assigned to**.
2.  Tap a name from the list that is displayed or enter text in the search field.
 Tap the send icon \(![Send icon.](../image/mobile_instances_send_droid.png)\) or **Submit** to save and submit your changes.

</td></tr><tr><td id="d445115e246">

**Edit**

</td><td>

1.  Tap **Edit**.
2.  Expand the fields and select an option from the list that is displayed or enter text in the Search field.
 Tap the send icon \(![Send icon](../image/mobile_instances_send_droid.png)\) or **Submit** to save and submit your changes.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>7.  Alternatively, to view the details of an open vulnerability record and to see more associated records and related lists, with the Assigned to My Groups screen displayed, tap a record on the list.

8.  On the open remediation task record that is displayed, choose one option from the following table to the view work notes or the related lists.

    ![Task record assigned to your group.](../image/mobile-vr-vg-record.jpg)

<table id="choicetable_mwd_ql2_4hb"><thead><tr><th align="left" id="d445115e306">

Option

</th><th align="left" id="d445115e309">

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td id="d445115e315">

**Details tab**

</td><td>

With the Details tab selected, review the fields of the RT record. Tap the menu icon \(![Top menu icon.](../image/mobile-top-menu.png)\) on the upper right of the screen to view the available options. The options that are displayed in this expanded menu vary. The remediation actions you can take on your vulnerable items vary and depend on the value that is displayed in the State field of the record. For more information about the State field and your remediation options, see [Vulnerability Response remediation task and vulnerable item states](../concept/vulnerabillity-states.md).

</td></tr><tr><td id="d445115e343">

**Activity Stream tab**

</td><td>

With the Activity Stream tab selected, choose one to continue.-   View the audit trail of work notes, activities, and additional comments of the record. Tap the plus icon \(![Plus icon.](../image/mobile_instances_plus.png)\) to add a work note or attach a file.
-   Tap the menu icon \(![Top menu icon.](../image/mobile-top-menu.png)\) on the upper right of the screen to view the available remediation options.


</td></tr><tr><td id="d445115e373">

**Related Lists**

</td><td>

With the Related Lists tab selected, view any of the related lists of the RT record that are populated. -   Tap **Vulnerable Items**. Tap a Vulnerable item \(VI\) record and navigate to the preferred solution record. Refer to the figure that follows the table for a navigation map and more details.
-   Tap **Change Requests**. View a list of the change requests that are associated with this record.
-   When displayed, tap the menu icon \(![Top menu icon.](../image/mobile-top-menu.png)\) on the upper right of a screen to view the available remediation options.


</td></tr></tbody>
</table>    The following image illustrates the navigation path from the remediation task record to the preferred solution record for the selected vulnerability. Navigate through the records and related lists to view solutions, deployment metrics, and items to address.

    ![Menu map from RT to solution](../image/mobile_VR-drill-down.png "Navigation path from remediation task through a Solution record")

9.  After reviewing, editing, or remediation, choose one to continue.

<table id="choicetable_ozz_n4g_phb"><thead><tr><th align="left" id="d445115e433">

Option

</th><th align="left" id="d445115e436">

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td id="d445115e442">

**Tap an icon on the bottom of the screen.**

</td><td>

On the bottom of the screen, choose one to continue.-   Tap the Vulnerability Response icon to return the landing screen.
-   If displayed, tap an icon to open another ServiceNow® mobile app.
-   Tap **Notification** to view notifications from the ServiceNow AI Platform and the Vulnerability Response Mobile app.
-   Tap **Settings** and choose one to continue.
    -   View information about the ServiceNow AI Platform instance on your device.
    -   Log out of the current ServiceNow AI Platform instance.


</td></tr></tbody>
</table>

```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\mobile-vr-login-mobileapp.md

```text
---
title: Log in to the Vulnerability Response Mobile app
description: Open the Vulnerability Response Mobile app and add a ServiceNow AI Platform instance with Vulnerability Response to your mobile device.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 3
breadcrumb: [Mobile experience for Vulnerability Response, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Log in to the Vulnerability Response Mobile app

Open the Vulnerability Response Mobile app and add a ServiceNow AI Platform instance with Vulnerability Response to your mobile device.

## Before you begin

Verify that you have completed the setup steps described in [Set up checklist for the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](mobile-vr-setupinstll-mobile.md).

![VR mobile app hierarchy.](../../security-operations/image/mobile_VR_overview-app.png "Vulnerability Response Mobile app")

Role required: Remediation owner \(sn\_vul.remediation\_owner\)

## About this task

Time to complete this task: 5-7 minutes.

## Procedure

1.  On your mobile device, tap the **ServiceNow Agent** app.

    ![ServiceNow Agent app.](../image/mobile-agentapp-NY.png)

    If you are not already logged in to a ServiceNow AI Platform instance, the Instances screen is displayed.

2.  If the ServiceNow AI Platform instance with the Vulnerability Response core application is not already added to your mobile device, follow these steps to add it.

    1.  On the Instances screen that is displayed, tap the plus icon \(![Plus icon.](../image/mobile_instances_plus.png)\).

        A screen is displayed that prompts you to enter and save an address of a ServiceNow AI Platform instance.

    2.  Fill in the fields.

<table id="simpletable_ar1_c3d_zhb"><thead><tr><th>

Field name

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Enter the instance address or scan a QR code.

</td><td>

Choose one to continue:-   Before the `.service-now.com`, enter the name of your ServiceNow AI Platform instance, for example, `vrmobilenewyorkagent`.
-   Enter an address. This address is the URL of your ServiceNow AI Platform instance that you want to view on your mobile device.
-   Scan a QR code.


</td></tr><tr><td>

\(Optional\) Enter nickname

</td><td>

Enter a nickname for this instance. If you have multiple instances added to the device, nicknames can help you quickly locate a ServiceNow AI Platform instance.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>    3.  Tap **Save and Login**

3.  On the ServiceNow login screen that is displayed, enter the User name and Password for the ServiceNow AI Platform instance that you want to add and tap **Login**.

    The Vulnerability Response landing screen is displayed. You have successfully logged in to the Vulnerability Response Mobile app !

4.  After you log in, verify notifications are enabled in the Vulnerability Response Mobile app.

    1.  On the landing screen on the bottom, tap **Notification**.

    2.  On the Notifications screen, tap **Enable Notifications** if not enabled.

        Notifications from the ServiceNow AI Platform are displayed on the Notifications screen in the Vulnerability Response Mobile app.

5.  After you login to an instance on your mobile device, it is the default ServiceNow AI Platform instance on the device.

    If you want to view another ServiceNow AI Platform on the device, you must log out of the default instance. To log out:

    1.  Tap the **Settings** icon.

    2.  Tap **Logout**.

        The Instances screen is displayed with any ServiceNow AI Platform instances you have added to the mobile device.

    3.  Tap another instance to log in to it, or, alternatively, follow the preceding steps to add another instance.


## Result

If the instances screen for the Vulnerability Response Mobile app is not displayed after you tap the **ServiceNow Agent** app on your device, verify that the **ServiceNow Agent** is permitted as a trusted app on your device. To permit access as a trusted app, navigate to the settings and general device management on your device and tap the option \(Trust app, etc.\) to permit access.

If an error message is displayed after you enter your credentials in the log in screen, verify that your User name and password for the ServiceNow AI Platform instance is correct.

If you have problems viewing the landing screen, verify your network connection.

If you cannot view the Instances screen after you tap the **ServiceNow Agent** app, try uninstalling it from your device. Verify you have the most current version of the app from the Apple iOS App Store or the Google Play Store and try reinstalling it.


```
