### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\vulnerability-response-common-content-pack.md

```text
---
title: Aggregated reports framework
description: Using aggregated reports framework, you can create reports for the data that involves complex and long-running on-the-fly queries for better performance.Finish the following sentence with a short statement on the business use case for this Solution. For example: This Analytics and Reporting Solution helps you track trends in incidents and plan your incident management strategy going forward.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Analytics and Reporting Solutions for Vulnerability Response, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Aggregated reports framework

Using aggregated reports framework, you can create reports for the data that involves complex and long-running on-the-fly queries for better performance.

The aggregated reports framework improves the performance by:

-   using lesser resources and rendering reports faster,
-   aggregating the data and reusing aggregations and breakdowns \(Group By\) and
-   reducing the queries to the database

When you have more than one million vulnerable items, use the aggregated reports framework provided in Vulnerability Response Common alongside the standard reports.

## Required roles

The following roles and tasks are associated with this solution:

-   ServiceNow AI Platform administrator \(admin\): Install and activate this Platform Analytics Solution and make any necessary changes to system properties.
-   Performance Analytics administrator \(pa\_admin\): Review the aggregations, widgets, and dashboards. Set up and start data collection. Share the dashboards with appropriate stakeholders.
-   Admin \(sn\_vul\_cmn.admin\): Create aggregations.
-   VR write\(sn\_vul\_cmn.write\): Edit the data in the Aggregations and Aggregated Results.
-   VR read \(sn\_vul\_cmn.read\): Review the data in the Aggregations and Aggregated Results.
-   Vulnerability analyst \(sn\_vul.vulnerability\_analyst\): Review Aggregated Results.

**Related topics**  


[Configure an aggregation for source data](../task/configure-indicator-for-source-data.md)

[Run multiple aggregations simultaneously](../task/run-multiple-aggregations.md)

[Configure the number of aggregations that can run simultaneously](../task/create-config-arf-processor.md)

[Create a report using an aggregation](../task/generate-report.md)


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\vulnerability-response-severity-mapping.md

```text
---
title: Severity mapping for Vulnerability Response
description: Vulnerability Response ships with National Vulnerability Database \(NVD\) to normalized ServiceNow severity mapping. ServiceNow third-party integrations provide severity mappings upon installation. These maps can be adjusted by changing the fields in existing maps.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Advanced Vulnerability Response configuration tasks, Vulnerability Response implementation, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Severity mapping for Vulnerability Response

Vulnerability Response ships with National Vulnerability Database \(NVD\) to normalized ServiceNow severity mapping. ServiceNow third-party integrations provide severity mappings upon installation. These maps can be adjusted by changing the fields in existing maps.

## Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration Severity Mapping

**Normalised\_Severity**

Calculate normalised\_severity on third-part entry using the severity coming from **sn\_vul\_nvd\_entry** table. This is set using Business Rules for Lookup normalized severity on **sn\_vul\_entry** table.

**Source\_severity**

Data from the **severity\_score** table is mapped to **source\_severity** table while the **Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration- API** is running.

**Priority**

This field is empty.

## Qualys Vulnerability Integration Severity Mapping

**Normalised\_Severity**

Calculate normalised\_severity on third-part entry using the severity coming from **sn\_vul\_nvd\_entry** table. This is set using Business Rules for Lookup normalized severity on **sn\_vul\_entry** table.

**Source\_severity**

Data from the **SEVERITY\_LEVEL** is mapped to the **source\_severity** table while the **Qualys Knowledge Base Integration** is running.

**Priority**

Data for the Priority field is obtained from **SEVERITY table** and mapped to **priority** table while **Qualys Host Detection Integration** is running using the Business Rule mapped to Qualys Data.

**Note:** Business Rules run in the background and checks the priority for Qualys and accordingly maps the Priority. So, to map Qualys data, BR is responsible.

## Tenable.io Vulnerability Integration Severity Mapping

**Normalised\_Severity**

Calculate normalised\_severity on third-part entry using the severity coming from **sn\_vul\_nvd\_entry** table. This is set using Business Rules for Lookup normalized severity on **sn\_vul\_entry** table.

**Source\_severity**

Data from **risk\_factor** table is mapped to **source\_severity** table while the **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

**Priority**

Data from **severity\_id** is mapped to **source\_severity** while the **Tenable.io Open Vulnerabilities Integration** is running.

**VPR**

Data from **score** is mapped to **Source\_risk\_score** while **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

Data from **Calculated from score** is mapped to **Source\_risk\_rating** while **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

## Tenable.sc Vulnerability Integration Severity Mapping

**Normalised\_Severity**

Calculate **normalised\_severity** on third-part entry using the severity coming from sn\_vul\_nvd\_entry table. This is set using Business Rules for Lookup normalized severity on **sn\_vul\_entry** table.

**Source\_severity**

Data for **riskFactor** table is mapped to **source\_severity** while the **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

**Priority**

Data from **severity** received as **"severity": \{ "id": "0", "name": "Info", "description": "Informative" \}** is mapped to **source\_severity** while the **Tenable.io Open Vulnerabilities Integration** is running.

**VPR**

Data from **vprScore** is mapped to **Source\_risk\_score** while **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

Data from **Calculated from vprScore** is mapped to **Source\_risk\_rating** while **Tenable.io Plugin Integration** is running.

## TVM Severity Mapping

**Normalised\_Severity**

Calculate normalised\_severity on third-part entry using the severuty coming from **sn\_vul\_nvd\_entry** table. This is set using Business Rules for Lookup normalized severity on **sn\_vul\_entry** table.

**Source\_severity**

Data from **severity** table is mapped to **source\_severity** while **Microsoft TVM Vulnerability\(CVE\) Integration** is running.

**Priority**

This field is empty.

**Parent Topic:**[Advanced Vulnerability Response configuration tasks](setup-tasks-optional.md)


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\vulnerabillity-states.md

```text
---
title: Vulnerability Response remediation task and vulnerable item states
description: With the Vulnerability Response application, you can use the state model to see the status of a remediation task, at any given time. Knowing how each state relates to and affects each other helps you to determine when and how to remediate your vulnerable items \(VIs\).
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Exploring the Vulnerability Response application, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Vulnerability Response remediation task and vulnerable item states

With the Vulnerability Response application, you can use the state model to see the status of a remediation task, at any given time. Knowing how each state relates to and affects each other helps you to determine when and how to remediate your vulnerable items \(VIs\).

## Remediation task states

Complex use cases can sometimes result in a vulnerable item being in a different state than its remediation task. Understanding how states work helps to explain this behavior and can help with creating remediation tasks and creating or editing remediation task rules.

**Note:** Starting with v23.0 of Vulnerability Response, the **Vulnerable Item State Approval** workflow is deprecated and replaced by the flow Vulnerability State Approval Change in the flow designer.

Remediation tasks have many possible states as shown in the following diagram.

![Vulnerability Response state flow diagram that describes the remediation task states as follows: Open, Under Investigation, Awaiting Implementation, In Review, Deferred or False Positive, Resolved, and Closed.](../image/VR-State-Flow-v10.3.png "Vulnerability Response state flow")

**Note:** Each task form contains **Follow** and **Update** buttons that are standard for ServiceNow tasks.

For more information on remediation task states and actions you can perform on it at a respective state, see [Vulnerability Response remediation task states](../reference/vr-rt-states.md).

For more information on detections, remediation task and vulnerable item states, see [Detections, remediation tasks, and vulnerable item states](vr-detections-rt-vi-states.md).

For more information on remediation task and VI states, see [Remediation tasks and vulnerable item states](vr-rt-vi-states.md).

For more information on remediation task state for VIs in multiple groups, see [Remediation task state for Vulnerable Items \(VITs\) in multiple groups](vr-rt-state-for-multi-grp-vi.md).

-   **[Vulnerable item age calculation and display](vr-vi-calcVI-age.md)**  
The age of vulnerable items \(VIs\) is displayed in the Vulnerability Response application with more detail.

**Parent Topic:**[Vulnerability Response](../reference/vuln-landing-page.md)


```

```text
---

# Vulnerability Response vulnerable item form fields

Vulnerable items are automatically created during third-party vulnerability integration imports.

## Vulnerable item fields

To view imported data in the fields listed in the following tables, you must have, at a minimum, the sn\_vul.read\_all role.

These fields are found on records listed in the Vulnerable Items \[sn\_vul\_vulnerable\_item\] table.

If you have enabled SAM NVD vulnerability scanning, the records are compared to the software in your Configuration Management Database \(CMDB\) and matches are found with vulnerable software or configuration items \(CIs\).

<table id="table_t4d_4bd_5s"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Select security tag

</td><td>

[Security tag](../../security-operations-common/task/create-class-group-and-tags.md) to add metadata to the record or identify who should have access to this security incident record. This field appears only after the vulnerable item has been saved.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Number

</td><td>

Automatically generated vulnerable item number for this record.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Source

</td><td>

Scanner that found this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Risk rating

</td><td>

Quantified **Risk Score** separating vulnerable items into Critical, High, Medium, Low, and None. For more information on risk ratings, see [Vulnerability Response calculators and vulnerability calculator rules](../concept/vuln-calculators-rules.md).**Note:** This base **Risk rating** isn’t the same as the Solution record **Risk rating.**

</td></tr><tr><td>

Risk score

</td><td>

Calculated amount of risk the vulnerable item poses to your environment.

**Note:** This base **Risk score** isn’t the same as the Solution record **Risk score.**

 For more information, see [Vulnerability Response calculators and vulnerability calculator rules](../concept/vuln-calculators-rules.md).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability

</td><td>

ID of the vulnerability associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Configuration item

</td><td>

Affected asset.

</td></tr><tr><td>

State

</td><td>

This field defaults to **Open**, but you can change it to **Under Investigation** if the vulnerability is ready for immediate remediation.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Until date for risk reduction

</td><td>

Date on which compensatory controls applied to the vulnerable item expire.**Note:** This field appears only when the Original risk score value is available.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Assignment group

</td><td>

Group selected to work on this remediation task.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Assigned to

</td><td>

Individual from the selected assignment group that works on this vulnerability.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Created

</td><td>

Date this vulnerable item was created in your instance.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Last opened

</td><td>

Date the vulnerable item was most recently opened in your instance. Initially, this is the same as the creation date of the vulnerable item, however, if it was closed, then reopened the Last opened date contains the date and time reopened.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Updated

</td><td>

Date of the last scan.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Age

</td><td>

Duration for which the VI has been active since the date it was last opened. If the VI is closed, the value of the Age field is set to zero.For more information on the Age field, see [Vulnerable item age calculation and display](../concept/vr-vi-calcVI-age.md). For more information on how to customize the calculation of Age duration, see the [KB1703270](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB1703270) article.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Vulnerability**

</td></tr><tr><td>

Summary

</td><td>

Description of the vulnerability.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Normalized degree of severity of this vulnerability. Severity maps are provided for NVD and with ServiceNow third-party integrations. For more information on creating or adjusting severity maps, see [Create a Vulnerability Response severity map](../task/create-severity-map.md).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability score \(v3\)

</td><td>

CVSS v3 score.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability score \(v2\)

</td><td>

CVSS v2 score.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Exploit exists

</td><td>

Yes, if at least one exploit is associated with the vulnerabilities associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Exploit attack vector

</td><td>

Most vulnerable attack vector of the exploits for the vulnerabilities associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Exploit skill level

</td><td>

Lowest skill level required to exploit the vulnerabilities associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Date published

</td><td>

Date the vulnerability was published.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Last modified

</td><td>

Date the vulnerability was last modified.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Threat

</td><td>

Relevant information about the threat. Pulled from the vulnerable entry record.**Note:** Any changes made here update the vulnerable entry record.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Remediation notes

</td><td>

Relevant solution to the threat, pulled from the vulnerable entry record.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Remediation** Available only with Vulnerability Solution Management.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Preferred solution

</td><td>

Preferred solution imported from the vulnerability record. **Note:** Any manual changes made here don’t change the vulnerability record and remain unchanged on the VI upon subsequent imports.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Summary

</td><td>

Imported Summary from the solution record.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Solution type

</td><td>

Potential or Preferred solution.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Initial Detection**

</td></tr><tr><td>

DNS name

</td><td>

Name of the Domain Name Service name. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

NetBIOS name

</td><td>

Name of the NetBIOS. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

IP Address

</td><td>

IPv4 or IPv6 address. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Port

</td><td>

Address of the port

</td></tr><tr><td>

Protocol

</td><td>

Name of the protocol.

</td></tr><tr><td>

SSL

</td><td>

Whether SSL encryption is used or not.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Detections**

</td></tr><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

State of the detection.

</td></tr><tr><td>

First found

</td><td>

Date the third-party source first found the detection on this asset.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Last found

</td><td>

Date the third-party source last found the detection on this asset.

</td></tr><tr><td>

DNS name

</td><td>

Name of the Domain Name Service name. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

NetBIOS name

</td><td>

Name of the NetBIOS. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

IP Address

</td><td>

IPv4 or IPv6 address. If a CI isn’t provided, this field is used to look up a matching CI, if one exists.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Port

</td><td>

Address of the port

</td></tr><tr><td>

Protocol

</td><td>

Name of the protocol.

</td></tr><tr><td>

SSL

</td><td>

Whether SSL encryption is used or not.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Times found

</td><td>

Number of times this vulnerability has been detected on this asset by the third-party source.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Integration run

</td><td>

Integration run that imported the detection.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Close** Available once the vulnerable item has been remediated and closed. Items in this section are read-only.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Closed by

</td><td>

Who closed the vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Closed

</td><td>

Date the vulnerable item was closed.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Close notes

</td><td>

Information included in the closure.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Age closed

</td><td>

Time period after which the VI was closed. If the VI is reopened, the value of the Age closed field is set to zero.For more information on how the Age closed field, see [Vulnerable item age calculation and display](../concept/vr-vi-calcVI-age.md). For more information on how to customize the calculation of Age closed duration, see the [KB1703270](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB1703270) article.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Notes**

</td></tr><tr><td>

Additional comments/Work notes

</td><td>

Any relevant information. Select the check box to add Additional comments.Starting with Vulnerability Response v20.0, you can add work notes in the Notes section for a deferred vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Activity

</td><td>

Only appears when a work note has been created.

</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">

**Related Links**

</td></tr><tr><td>

Calculate Risk Score

</td><td>

When either the **Vulnerability Severity** or **Risk Score** calculators is enabled, the **Risk Score** field is updated.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>The following are the vulnerable items related lists.

<table id="table_bmj_3ky_fv"><thead><tr><th>

Related List

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Remediation Tasks

Tasks associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Affecting Tasks

</td><td>

Tasks associated with this vulnerable item.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Associated IP Addresses

</td><td>

Available if the Qualys Vulnerability Integration Vulnerability Integration application is installed.IP addresses that are found during de-duplication.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Impacted Services

</td><td>

Business services impacted by this vulnerable item. Shown when this information is available in the `cmdb` record. This information can be entered manually or using the ServiceNow® Service Mapping application. See [Service Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/it-operations-management/service-mapping/c_ServiceMappingOverview.md) for more information.

 If an affected CI associated with the vulnerable item is added or updated, information in this related list is automatically updated when the record is saved.

Status of change requests and other approval information associated with the VIT.

</td></tr><tr><td>

</td><td>
```

```text
---
title: Configure watchdog

locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Additional Vulnerability Response setup and configuration tasks, Vulnerability Response implementation, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Configure watchdog

Use the watchdog configuration page to create new or update existing watchdogs with conditions that you want to specify. You can enable the notification for each watchdog, and get notified when the conditions are met.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_sec\_cmn.admin

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **Workspaces** &gt; **Security Exposure Management Workspace**.

2.  Select **Administration** in the navigation pane.

3.  Select **Review** on the Watchdogs tile.

4.  Select the watchdog that you want to implement.

5.  On the form, fill in the fields.

    |Field|Description|
    |-----|-----------|
    |Name|Name of the watchdog.|
    |Active|Set the check box to **true** to enable the watchdog.|
    |Condition type|Choose from Filter or Script.|
    |Advanced condition|This field appears only when Script is selected from Condition type.|
    |Table|Select table to run the watchdog condition.|
    |Condition|Condition to evaluate the watchdog.|
    |Notify|Check box to notify the groups or users if the watchdog condition is met.|
    |Users|List of users to be informed if the watchdog condition is met.|
    |Groups|Group to be informed if the watchdog condition is met.|
    |Watch log|
    |Watchdog|Reference to the watchdog table.|
    |Count|Number of records that meet the watchdog criteria.|
    |Notes|Additional notes for the watchdog logs.|

6.  Select **Update**.



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\watchdog-overview.md

```text
---
title: Watchdog for Vulnerability Response
description: The watchdog is used to track tables based on the conditions you specify. For example, it can be used to log details of integration run failures in Vulnerability Response.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Exploring the Vulnerability Response application, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Watchdog for Vulnerability Response

The watchdog is used to track tables based on the conditions you specify. For example, it can be used to log details of integration run failures in Vulnerability Response.

You can choose to be notified about these failures. Based on this notification, you can plan the remediation. The default watchdogs are:

-   Integration run failed: If the integration run State is set to Complete and Substate is set to Failed, users and user groups are notified if the condition returns a count greater than 0, that is, the integration run fails.
-   Long running integrations: Script to verify if the integration run is exceeding the set time. This watchdog proactively alerts you whenever there is a delay in the completion of the integration run.
-   Overlapping periodic runs: Script to check the recurring integration runs that still continue to run during their upcoming occurrence. It generates a link that contains the list of these integrations runs.

## Watch log

The watchdog comes with a watch log, which provides a count of the number of times the condition has been met.

## Overview dashboard

Use the Overview dashboard to monitor the watchdog for the past 30 days. You can create multiple widgets to track other watchdog topics you are monitoring.

Starting with version 19.0 of Vulnerability Response, this dashboard is available in the New Experience UI.

**Note:** If you are on Tokyo, you can view the dashboards in the Next Experience UI but with some functional loss.

<table id="id_fm5_qkw_5sb"><tbody><tr><td>

Name

</td><td>

Description

</td></tr><tr><td>

Last 30 days WatchDog failure

</td><td>

All the conditions met in the past 30 days.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>For more information, see [Configure watchdog](../task/watchdog-configure.md) and [Error handling for detections](../reference/detections-error-handling.md).


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\wiz-activate-backfill.md

```text
---
title: Schedule the Wiz Vulnerability Integrations
description: You can schedule the integration import times on the their records on the Vulnerability Integrations \[sn\_vul\_int\_fw\_integration\] table and launch them on-demand.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Configure, Wiz Vulnerability Response Integration, Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Schedule the Wiz Vulnerability Integrations

You can schedule the integration import times on the their records on the Vulnerability Integrations \[sn\_vul\_int\_fw\_integration\] table and launch them on-demand.

## Before you begin

Starting with v1.1, the Missing Assets \[sn\_vul\_wiz\_missing\_asset\] table for storing assets from the backfill integrations is deprecated. After upgrading to version 1.1, you must backdate your primary Wiz integrations by three days and run them.

**Note:** Activating the **Wiz Asset Integration** is optional. You are no longer required to schedule or run it so it runs before the other integrations.

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Integrations**.

2.  Select an integration record in the **Name** column to open it.

3.  Set how often, when you want to run the integration, and any conditions you want on **Schedule** tab.

4.  Select **Update** to save your changes.

5.  Select **Execute Now** to run the integration on-demand.

    Asset data is imported and discovered item records are created or updated in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance based on the Resource types that you selected on the [Configuration tab](wiz-assets-resources-tab.md).

    -   The Asset Integration imports data about your cloud assets reported by the Wiz scanner.
    -   Host vulnerability findings are mapped to host vulnerable items \(VITs\).
    -   Host test results that are associated with the resource type, VIRTUAL MACHINE are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
    -   Container vulnerability findings are mapped to container vulnerable items \(CVITs\).
    -   Configuration test results findings are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
    -   Issues findings are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
    -   The Backfill Integrations import and process any Asset IDs that are reported as missing by the primary vulnerability and compliance integrations.
    The backfill integrations are activated automatically after their primary integrations are successfully completed. See [Wiz Backfill Integrations](../concept/wiz-backfill.md) for more information.



```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\wiz-assets-resources-tab.md

```text
---
title: Identify Wiz Resource types for the Wiz Vulnerability Integrations
description: Set filtering on the Wiz integration configuration page so that you import only the Resource Types \(assets\) reported by Wiz that you want.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: task
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 5
breadcrumb: [Configure, Wiz Vulnerability Response Integration, Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Identify Wiz Resource types for the Wiz Vulnerability Integrations

Set filtering on the Wiz integration configuration page so that you import only the Resource Types \(assets\) reported by Wiz that you want.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  Select the **Resource Type Configuration** tab and select the **Unlock Resource types** icon \(![A padlock icon that shows the field is locked and not editable.](../../../security-incident-response/image/lock-icon.png)\).

3.  With the Resource types field displayed, select the **Add/Remove multiple** icon \(![Icon to open a modal](../../../secops-analyst-workspace/image/icon-conference-call.png)\) the third icon from the top to the right of the Resources types field.

    The Edit Members modal is displayed.

4.  Select one or more resources from the **Available Wiz Resource Types** field and select the right arrow \(![Expand right](../../../../reuse/icons/product-icons/chevron-right-outline-24.svg)\) to move them to the **Selected Wiz Resource Types** field.

    **Note:** The resource types you configure on the configuration tab apply to all the Wiz integrations except the Wiz Container Vulnerability Integration. The Wiz Container Vulnerability Integration works with discovered container image records, not discovered item records.

    At a minimum, you must add either the `VIRTUAL_MACHINE` or `SERVERLESS` resource type for the Host Vulnerability Integration. You can add both. For the Host Test Results Integration, you must, at a minimum, add the `VIRTUAL_MACHINE` resource type.

5.  Select **Save**.

    The **Resource Type Configuration** tab is displayed. Your saved resources are displayed on the tab.

6.  Select the lock **Resource types** icon \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\).

7.  Select **Save** to save your edits.

8.  Select **Save and test** if you’re ready to validate your credentials.

    If your credentials are successfully saved and validated, a message is displayed.

    After you validate and test, you have the option to set more filtering parameters on the integration instance records. With this filtering, you can further limit imports to only the specific resource types that you want to import for the Host Vulnerability and Test Results integrations.

    **Note:**

    If you configure resource types on the Resource Type Configuration tab, and you choose to configure parameters on the integration instance records, your configurations on integration instance take precedence over your settings on the Resource Type Configuration tab.

    For example, if you add **VIRTUAL\_MACHINE** on the **Resource Type Configuration Tab**, and you also configure **SERVERLESS** for the Host Vulnerability integration instance, the Host Vulnerability integration will import only the SERVERLESS Resource Type.

    If you add **VIRTUAL\_MACHINE** and **SERVERLESS** resource types on the **Resource Type Configuration Tab**, and you also configure **SERVERLESS** for the Host Vulnerability integration instance, the Host Vulnerability integration will import only the SERVERLESS Resource Type.

9.  To add more filter parameters, follow these steps on the integration instance records.

    1.  To navigate to the Integration Instance \[sn\_sec\_int\_impl\] table, enter `sn_sec_int_impl.LIST` in the navigation panel.

    2.  Select the **Vulnerability Response Integration with Wiz** record to open it.

    3.  On the **Integration Instance Parameters** tab, enter one of the following parameters into the **Search** field under the Configuration column.

        -   `host_asset_type`
        -   `cc_resource_type`
    4.  Select the information icon \(![A symbol for information indicates that additional details are available.](../../../vulnerability-response/image/info-icon.png)\) next to the record link to open the record.

        **Note:** You might have to select the link to change the scope for the record.

        The Integration Parameter record is displayed.

    5.  Enter the resources that you want to import in the **Default value** field for the respective integration.

        You can enter values only for the Host Vulnerability and Test Results Integrations. These values must also be on the **Resource Type Configuration** tab that you entered in the previous steps.

        For example, for the Host Integration, enter any values in the **host\_asset\_type** field that you entered on the **Resource Type Configuration** tab for this integration.

        For the Test Results Integration, enter any values in the **cc\_resource\_type** field that you entered on the **Resource Type Configuration** tab for this integration.

    6.  Select **Update** to save your changes.

        The Integration Instance record is displayed.

        You have the option to limit the number of assets you import with each integration run for the Wiz Asset Integration.

10. To modify the number of records processed per Integration Process for the Asset Integration, follow these steps.

    1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Admin** &gt; **Integrations**.

    2.  Select the **Wiz Asset Integration** record to open it.

        This integration is activated by default. The default setting for imported records is 500.

    3.  Select the information icon next to the **Source Instance** field to open the integration instance record.

    4.  In the **Search** field with the **Integration Instance Parameters** tab selected, enter the `asset_record_count` parameter.

    5.  Select the record in the Configuration column to open it.

    6.  Verify that you are in the proper scope to edit the record.

    7.  Double-click the **Value** field to modify it.

    8.  Select **Update** to save your changes.


## What to do next

After you configure all of the integrations, schedule the **Wiz Asset Integration** or initiate it on-demand so it runs before the other integrations. This is the only integration that can discover, import, and create discovered item records for Wiz data. The other integrations depend on imported data from the Wiz Asset Integration. The Wiz Container Vulnerability Integration imports and processes discovered container image records.

See [schedule Wiz integrations](wiz-activate-backfill.md) for more information about scheduling and activating the Wiz integrations and backfill integrations on-demand.

To set filtering for the integrations, for more information see:

-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Host Vulnerabilities Integration](wiz-host-tab-filters.md)
-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Container Vulnerabilities Integration](wiz-container-tab-filters.md)
-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Test Results Integration](wiz-test-result-tab-filters.md)
-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Test Results Integration](wiz-test-result-tab-filters.md)
-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Issues Integration](wiz-issues-tab-filters.md)
-   [Set filtering for the Wiz Host Test Results Integration](wiz-host-test-result-tab-filters.md)


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\wiz-backfill.md

```text
---
title: Wiz Backfill Integrations
description: Prior to v1.1, the Wiz Backfill integrations import and process details for your Assets IDs that are flagged as missing by the primary vulnerability integrations. Starting with v1.1, the backfill integrations are deprecated.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Configure, Wiz Vulnerability Response Integration, Vulnerability Response integrations, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Wiz Backfill Integrations

Prior to v1.1, the Wiz Backfill integrations import and process details for your Assets IDs that are flagged as missing by the primary vulnerability integrations. Starting with v1.1, the backfill integrations are deprecated.

## Upgrading to version 1.1

Starting with v1.1, the Missing Assets \[sn\_vul\_wiz\_missing\_asset\] table for storing assets from the backfill integrations is deprecated. After upgrading to version 1.1, you must backdate your primary Wiz integrations by three days and run them.

The Wiz Backfill Integrations are activated by default.

After you upgrade, the following backfill integrations are no longer required. The primary integrations can create discovered item records if they are not found.

-   Host Vulnerability Backfill Integration
-   Test Results Backfill Integration
-   Host Test Results Backfill Integration
-   Issues Backfill Integration

## Backfill Integrations overview \(for versions prior to v1.1\)

A backfill integration is a specialized Wiz integration that processes asset IDs that are reported as missing by the primary Wiz integration imports. Asset IDs flagged as missing, that is, their discovered item records are not found, are stored on the Wiz Missing Assets \[sn\_vul\_wiz\_missing\_asset\] table.

The following Wiz Backfill integrations are activated automatically after their corresponding primary Wiz vulnerability integrations are successfully completed. These integrations process the missing Asset IDs based on their integration type that are stored on the Missing Assets table.

|Primary integration|Backfill integration|Integration type|
|-------------------|--------------------|----------------|
|Host Vulnerability Integration|Host Vulnerability Backfill Integration|host\_vr|
|Test Results Integration|Test Results Backfill Integration|test\_results|
|Host Test Results Integration|Host Test Results Backfill Integration|host\_test\_results|
|Issues Integration|Issues Backfill Integration|issues|

**Note:** The Wiz Asset Integration does not have a backfill integration, because it can discover assets and create and update discovered item records on the Discovered item \[sn\_sec\_cmn\_src\_ci\] table. The Wiz Container Vulnerability Integration imports and processes discovered container image records.

For example, the Wiz Host Vulnerability Backfill Integration is activated automatically after the Wiz Host Vulnerability Integration is successfully completed. The backfill integration imports and processes any Asset IDs \(type host\_vr\) that are not found by the Host Vulnerability Integration.

If discovered items are found for these Asset IDs by the backfill integration, they are reconciled with their corresponding records and processed. If a corresponding discovered item record is not found, the backfill integration skips these assets, and they remain stored on the Missing Assets table. After records on the Wiz Missing Assets table are successfully reconciled with discovered item records, they are removed from the Missing Assets table.

The backfill integrations by default import and process 100 records on the Wiz Missing Assets table for their corresponding primary Wiz vulnerability integrations. If more than 100 records are found by a backfill integration, an additional integration run for the backfill integration is automatically initiated to process the remaining records.

For example, if the Test Results Integration runs and reports 120 missing Asset IDs, these are stored on the Wiz Missing Asset table. The Test Results Backfill integration is automatically activated to process 100 records for type, test\_results. After this Test Results Backfill Integration run is completed, an additional run for the Test Results Backfill Integration is automatically activated to process the remaining 20 records.

## Schedule and activate the integrations on-demand

See [Schedule the Wiz Vulnerability Integrations](../task/wiz-activate-backfill.md) for more information about activating the integrations on-demand and scheduling integration runs.


```

```text
---
title: Set filtering for the Wiz Container Vulnerabilities Integration
description: Set the filtering values to import the container vulnerability data that you want.

# Set filtering for the Wiz Container Vulnerabilities Integration

Set the filtering values to import the container vulnerability data that you want.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  Select the **Container Vulnerabilities Configuration** tab.

3.  Fill in the fields.

    For some fields, you can specify multiple values. `--None--` is the \(default\). If `--None--` remains selected for a field, no data is imported for this field.

    If displayed select the lock icons \(![An closed padlock icon that indicates the field is locked and not editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-lock-icon.png)\) and \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\) to edit and lock your edits.

<table id="table_wdj_klr_qfc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

First

</td><td>

Pagination. Enter a value. You might prefer to start with 1000.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has Public Exploit

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available exploit: \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Subscription

</td><td>

Import findings from the following strings for external subscription IDs: \(AWS Account, Azure Subscription, GCP Project, and OCI Compartment\). If you do not provide a value, all subscriptions are returned.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has Fix

</td><td>

Import vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available fix \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability

</td><td>

Import vulnerability findings with matching external ID\(s\), for example, CVE-1234-5678,CVE-9110-26117.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has CISA KEV Exploit

</td><td>

Import only vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available CISA KEV exploit \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Project ID

</td><td>

Import only vulnerability findings for the given projects \(strings\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has High Privileges

</td><td>

Return only vulnerability findings that have high privileges \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Status

</td><td>

Return only findings with these statuses. You can specify multiple values:-   --None--
-   Active
-   Error
-   Inactive


</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has Admin Privileges

</td><td>

Return only vulnerability findings that have admin privileges. \(true/false\)

</td></tr><tr><td>

Detection Method

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings found by these detection methods:-   --None--
-   DEFAULT\_PACKAGE
-   FILE\_PATH
-   INSTALLED\_PROGRAM
-   INSTALLED\_PROGRAM\_BY\_SERVICE
-   LIBRARY
-   OS
-   PACKAGE


</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has Limited Internet Exposure

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings that have low internet exposure-   --None--
-   TRUE
-   FALSE


</td></tr><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

Filter findings by status:-   --None--
-   OPEN
-   REJECTED
-   RESOLVED


</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability Severity

</td><td>

Filter findings by vulnerability severity:-   --None--
-   CRITICAL
-   HIGH
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM


</td></tr><tr><td>

Validated In Runtime

</td><td>

-   --None--
-   Yes- Select **Yes** to pull in data for resources that have this flag set to `Yes` in the Runtime field. In Wiz console, the 'Validated in Runtime' status for a finding typically persists for a 48-hour period from the last time the vulnerable package was detected in memory.
-   No. Do not pull data for these resources.


</td></tr></tbody>
</table>4.  Starting with v32.0 \(USEM\) and 4.0 \(Core\), the Path Column in the Container Image Package \[sn\_vul\_container\_image\_package\] table for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Wiz third-party integrations is deprecated.

    Path tracking has been moved to the Path Column on the Container Image Finding \[sn\_vul\_container\_image\_ﬁndings\] table to support accurate and consistent path and image association. If you use these integrations you must refer to the Path column available on the Container Image Finding \[sn\_vul\_container\_image\_ﬁndings\] table.

5.  **Select Save and test**.

    If the credentials have been saved and validated successfully a message is displayed. You can select filtering for another integration import.

6.  [Configure container image granularity keys for Container Vulnerability Response](../../../container-vulnerability-response/task/cvr-configure-key-granularity.md).

    You can configure the granularity of the container vulnerable items \(CVITs\) by specifying the key combination. For more information about supported keys, system properties, and the scheduled job also required, see [Configuring container image granularity keys for Container Vulnerability Response](../../../container-vulnerability-response/concept/cvr-configuring-findings-key-granularity.md).

    A CVIT is created by default by combining the image repository, vulnerability, and image. You can add components to the key for further granularity. For example, create a CVIT is for a combination of image repository, vulnerability, image, and cluster.



```

```text
# Set filtering for the Wiz Host Vulnerabilities Integration

Set the filtering values to import the host vulnerability data that you want.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  If not already selected, select the **Host Vulnerabilities Configuration** tab.

3.  Fill in the fields.

    These fields that are displayed provide you with filters to import basic host vulnerability data. Refer to the following tables for more information.

<table id="table_x52_pjn_bgc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

First

</td><td>

Pagination. Enter a value. You might prefer to start with 1000.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Finding severity. None is the default. If selected, this indicates you don’t want to import any data for this field. You can specify multiple values:-   NONE - Return assets with no available severity values.
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM
-   HIGH
-   CRITICAL


</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Status

</td><td>

Return only vulnerability findings for assets with these statuses. You can specify multiple values:-   None \(default\) - If selected, this indicates you do not want to import any data for this field.
-   Active
-   Error
-   Inactive


</td></tr></tbody>
</table>    To view more filtering options, select the **Advanced** check box.

4.  Select the Advanced check box to view more filtering options.

    For most fields, you can specify multiple values. `--None--` is the \(default\). If `--None--` remains selected for a field, no data is imported for this field.

    If displayed select the lock icons \(![An closed padlock icon that indicates the field is locked and not editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-lock-icon.png)\) and \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\) to edit and lock your edits.

<table id="table_wdj_klr_qfc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Detection Method

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings found by these detection methods:-   --None--
-   DEFAULT\_PACKAGE
-   FILE\_PATH
-   INSTALLED\_PROGRAM
-   INSTALLED\_PROGRAM\_BY\_SERVICE
-   LIBRARY
-   OS
-   PACKAGE


</td></tr><tr><td>

Vulnerability

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings with matching external ID\(s\) that you enter, for example, CVE-1234-5678,CVE-9110-26117.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

Filter by finding status:-   --None--
-   OPEN
-   REJECTED
-   RESOLVED


</td></tr><tr><td>

Related Issue Severity

</td><td>

-   --None--
-   CRITICAL
-   HIGH
-   INFORMATIONAL
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM


</td></tr><tr><td>

Has Public Exploit

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available exploit: \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Project ID

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings with strings that you enter for the given projects.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has Fix

</td><td>

Filter on vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available fix \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource

</td><td>

Filter on a resource you enter.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has Admin Privileges

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings for assets that have admin privileges \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Subscription

</td><td>

Import findings from the following strings for external subscriptions: \(AWS Account, Azure Subscription, GCP Project, and OCI Compartment\). You can specify multiple values in an array. If you do not provide a value, all subscriptions are returned.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has CISA KEV Exploit

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings for vulnerabilities with an available CISA KEV exploit \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has High Privileges

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings for assets that have high privileges \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Validated In Runtime

</td><td>

-   --None--
-   Yes- Select **Yes** to pull in data for resources that have this flag set to `Yes` in the Runtime field. In Wiz console, the 'Validated in Runtime' status for a finding typically persists for a 48-hour period from the last time the vulnerable package was detected in memory.
-   No. Do not pull data for these resources.


</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has Wide Internet Exposure

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings for assets that have high internet exposure \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Has Limited Internet Exposure

</td><td>

Filter for vulnerability findings for assets that have low internet exposure \(true/false\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

First Seen At \(After\)

</td><td>

Filter by assets seen starting with a date you select.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resolved At \(After\)

</td><td>

Filter by assets with vulnerabilities that have been resolved starting with a date you select.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Updated At \(After\)

</td><td>

Filter by assets that have been updated starting with a date you select.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>5.  **Select Save and test**.

    If the credentials have been saved and validated successfully a message is displayed. You can select filtering for another integration import.
```

```text
# Set filtering for the Wiz Host Test Results Integration

Set the filtering values to import Wiz host test results.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  Select the **Host Test Results Configuration** tab.

3.  Fill in the fields.

    For some fields, you can specify multiple values. `--None--` is the \(default\). If `--None--` remains selected for a field, no data is imported for this field.

    If displayed select the lock icons \(![An closed padlock icon that indicates the field is locked and not editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-lock-icon.png)\) and \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\) to edit and lock your edits.

    Select the **Add/Remove multiple** icon \(![Icon to open a modal](../../../secops-analyst-workspace/image/icon-conference-call.png)\) to open the Edit members modal.

<table id="table_mhn_551_sgc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

Filter by status:-   --None--
-   OPEN
-   REJECTED
-   RESOLVED


</td></tr><tr><td>

Cloud platform

</td><td>

Configuration findings by Cloud platform. You can specify multiple values, for example, AWS, GitHub, Terraform, OpenAI, GKE, OKE, EKS, AKS.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Choose one for Severity filtering:-   --None--
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM
-   HIGH
-   CRITICAL
-   NONE - Return findings with no available severity values.
You might prefer to start with HIGH or CRITICAL to limit your import to findings of critical importance.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Status

</td><td>

Specify by status:-   --None--
-   Active
-   Error
-   Inactive


</td></tr><tr><td>

Framework Category

</td><td>

Enter security frameworks, security subcategories, or security categories. You can specify multiple values.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource type

</td><td>

Select **Add/Remove multiple** icon to display the modal. Move your choices to the right column and select **Save**.Or, search for an asset target record type, for example, FIREWALL. You also have the open to add a new resource type record.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Native type

</td><td>

Specify findings based on the native type of the cloud resource, for example, `bucket`.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Subscription ID

</td><td>

Specify by entering subscription ID. You can specify multiple values in an array.

</td></tr><tr><td>

First

</td><td>

Pagination. Enter a value. You might prefer to start with 500.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>4.  **Select Save and test**.

    If the credentials have been saved and validated successfully a message is displayed. You can select filtering for another integration import.



```

```text
## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  Select the **Issues Configuration** tab.

3.  Fill in the fields.

    For some fields, you can specify multiple values. `--None--` is the \(default\). If `--None--` remains selected for a field, no data is imported for this field.

    If displayed select the lock icons \(![An closed padlock icon that indicates the field is locked and not editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-lock-icon.png)\) and \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\) to edit and lock your edits.

<table id="table_wdj_klr_qfc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Filter by finding severity.-   --None--
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM
-   HIGH
-   CRITICAL
-   INFORMATIONAL


</td></tr><tr><td>

Project ID

</td><td>

Import only findings for the given projects \(strings\).

</td></tr><tr><td>

Issue type

</td><td>

Issues by the rule type they are generated by.-   CLOUD\_CONFIGURATION
-   THREAT\_DETECTION
-   TOXIC\_COMBINATION


</td></tr><tr><td>

Subscription ID

</td><td>

Specify findings by entering a subscription ID. You can specify multiple values in an array.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Cloud platform

</td><td>

Configuration findings by Cloud platform. You can specify multiple values, for example, AWS, GitHub, Terraform, OpenAI, GKE, OKE, EKS, AKS.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Framework Category

</td><td>

Enter security frameworks, security subcategories, or security categories. You can specify multiple values.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Native type

</td><td>

Issues based on the native type of the cloud resource, for example, `bucket`.

</td></tr><tr><td>

First

</td><td>

Pagination. Enter a value. You might prefer to start with 1000.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

Filter by status:-   --None--
-   OPEN
-   IN PROGRESS
-   REJECTED
-   RESOLVED


</td></tr><tr><td>

Fetch rejected findings

</td><td>

Select this check box if you want to import Cloud test results in a failed state \(rejected\) from Wiz.

**Note:** These findings are mapped to CTRs and labeled with `Wiz Issues` as the source to help you track them.

If not selected, failed test results reported by Wiz are not imported.If imported, these test results remain in a failed state but are not rolled up to Configuration Compliance findings.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Close rejected findings

</td><td>

Select this check box if you want to import failed Cloud test results reported by Wiz and automatically close them after import. If not selected, these test results remain in a failed state but are neither closed nor rolled up to Configuration Compliance findings.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>4.  **Select Save and test**.

    If the credentials have been saved and validated successfully a message is displayed. You can select filtering for another integration import.



```

```text
Set the filtering values to import the cloud test results data that you want.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_wiz.configure\_integration

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Wiz Vulnerability Integration** &gt; **Administration** &gt; **Configuration**.

2.  Select the **Test Results Configuration** tab.

3.  Fill in the fields.

    For some fields, you can specify multiple values. `--None--` is the \(default\). If `--None--` remains selected for a field, no data is imported for this field.

    **Note:** Host configurations from Wiz are not imported.

    If displayed select the lock icons \(![An closed padlock icon that indicates the field is locked and not editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-lock-icon.png)\) and \(![An opened padlock icon that indicates the field is unlocked and editable.](../../../data-loss-prevention/image/dlp-unlock-icon.png)\) to edit and lock your edits.

    Select the **Add/Remove multiple** icon \(![Icon to open a modal](../../../secops-analyst-workspace/image/icon-conference-call.png)\) to open the Edit members modal.

<table id="table_wdj_klr_qfc"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Severity

</td><td>

Choose one for Severity filtering:-   --None--
-   LOW
-   MEDIUM
-   HIGH
-   CRITICAL
-   NONE - Return findings with no available severity values.
You might prefer to start with HIGH or CRITICAL to limit your import to findings of critical importance.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Project ID

</td><td>

Specify only Cloud Configuration findings for Projects, for example, `Named "Project ID"`.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Cloud platform

</td><td>

Configuration findings by Cloud platform. You can specify multiple values, for example, AWS, GitHub, Terraform, OpenAI, GKE, OKE, EKS, AKS.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Subscription ID

</td><td>

Specify by entering subscription ID. You can specify multiple values in an array.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource Status

</td><td>

Specify by status:-   --None--
-   Active
-   Error
-   Inactive


</td></tr><tr><td>

Framework Category

</td><td>

Enter security frameworks, security subcategories, or security categories. You can specify multiple values.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Resource type

</td><td>

Select **Add/Remove multiple** icon to display the modal. Move your choices to the right column and select **Save**.Or, search for an asset target record type, for example, FIREWALL. You also have the open to add a new resource type record.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Native type

</td><td>

Specify findings based on the native type of the cloud resource, for example, `bucket`.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Has remediation

</td><td>

Specify findings with \(`TRUE`\), without \(`FALSE`\) remediation instructions, or --None--.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Status

</td><td>

Filter by status:-   --None--
-   OPEN
-   REJECTED
-   RESOLVED


</td></tr><tr><td>

First

</td><td>

Pagination. Enter a value. You might prefer to start with 500.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Fetch rejected findings

</td><td>

Select this check box if you want to import Cloud test results in a failed state \(rejected\) from Wiz. If not selected, failed Cloud test results reported by Wiz are not imported.If imported, these test results remain in a failed state but are not rolled up to Configuration Compliance findings.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Close rejected findings

</td><td>

Select this check box if you want to import failed Cloud test results reported by Wiz and automatically close them after import. If not selected, these test results remain in a failed state but are neither closed nor rolled up to Configuration Compliance findings.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>4.  **Select Save and test**.
```

```text
is\_ignoredif status is Rejected, is\_ignored is set to true, else false.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>## Mapping to vulnerable item and test result records

-   The Asset Integration imports data about your cloud assets reported by the Wiz scanner.
-   Host vulnerability findings are mapped to host vulnerable items \(VITs\).
-   Host test results that are associated with the resource type, VIRTUAL MACHINE are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
-   Container vulnerability findings are mapped to container vulnerable items \(CVITs\).
-   Configuration test results findings are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
-   Issues findings are mapped to cloud test results records \(CTRs\) in the Configuration Compliance application.
-   The Backfill Integrations import and process any Asset IDs that are reported as missing by the primary vulnerability and compliance integrations.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\working-unmatched-cis.md

```text
---
title: Working with unmatched CIs
description: As a Vulnerability Manager and Analyst, you can view and reclassify unmatched Configuration Items \(CIs\), reconcile unmatched discovered items, reapply CI lookup rules on the selected discovered items, and de-duplicate existing CIs.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1
breadcrumb: [Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Working with unmatched CIs

As a Vulnerability Manager and Analyst, you can view and reclassify unmatched Configuration Items \(CIs\), reconcile unmatched discovered items, reapply CI lookup rules on the selected discovered items, and de-duplicate existing CIs.

See the CI matching in Vulnerability Response knowledge base article \[[KB0998706\]](https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0998706) for more information about CI matching rule concepts and how CI matching works.

-   **[View and reclassify unmatched configuration items](../task/view-discovered-items.md)**  
Configuration items \(CIs\) that are not found in the Configuration Management Database \(CMDB\) are placed in a viewable list of discovered items. This list offers a convenient way to reclassify unmatched CIs.
-   **[Reconcile unmatched discovered items](../task/reapply-reconcile-unmatched-discovered-items.md)**  
Create a scheduled job to reconcile unmatched discovered items.
-   **[Steps to help prevent duplicate or orphaned records after running Vulnerability Response CI lookup rules](../../security-operations-common/concept/ci-identifier-rules-impl-test.md)**  
Take steps to help prevent duplicate or orphan records resulting from matching \(configuration items \(CIs\) within the CMDB.
-   **[De-duplicating existing configuration items](reapply-deduplication.md)**  
Whenever configuration items \(CIs\) are updated through a deduplication task, the discovered items \(DIs\) that are related to those CIs are also updated. The vulnerable items \(VIs\) and detections are also updated with the CI.
-   **[Resolve remediation tasks](../task/remediate-vuln-groups.md)**  
The flexibility inherent in Vulnerability Response allows you to remediate vulnerabilities in whatever way suits your security organization.

**Parent Topic:**[Vulnerability Response remediation overview](cj-common-vuln-tasks.md)


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\working-with-exception-rule.md

```text
---
title: Working with an exception rule
description: You can request an exception for vulnerabilities, vulnerable items \(VIs\), or configuration items \(CIs\) that can't be remediated or deferred immediately. By automating the VI deferral process, you can defer the matching VIs based on the rule when the system identifies them.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
breadcrumb: [Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Working with an exception rule

You can request an exception for vulnerabilities, vulnerable items \(VIs\), or configuration items \(CIs\) that can't be remediated or deferred immediately. By automating the VI deferral process, you can defer the matching VIs based on the rule when the system identifies them.

You can perform the following tasks for an exception rule:

-   [Create an exception rule](../task/create-exception-rule.md)
-   [Approve an exception rule request](../task/approve-ex-rule-request.md)
-   [Activating an exception rule](activating-ex-rule.md)
-   [Reopen an exception rule](../task/reopen-ex-rule.md)
-   [Update an approved exception rule](../task/update-approved-ex-rule.md)
-   [Delete an exception rule](../task/delete-ex-rule.md)

**Note:**

Starting from v21.0 of Vulnerability Response, you can configure the time frames for approving false positives and exceptions, along with email notifications for both the approver and requester after a set number of days. When a request is raised, the vulnerable item changes to In-Review status and a state change record is created. If the approver doesn't respond within the configured time frame, the vulnerable item or remediation task reverts to Open status. The previous state is stored in the **backup\_state** field. For more information, see [Configure approval rules for Exception Management](../task/exception-mgt-approval-rules.md).


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response\working-with-retired-CIs.md

```text
---
title: Working with retired configuration items
description: Decommissioned configuration items \(CIs\) are moved to retired, archived, or deleted state in the Configuration Management Database \(CMDB\). Vulnerability Response contains vulnerable items \(VIs\) that are made up of CIs. When the state of a CI is updated to retired, the associated VIs are closed with the substate 'CI decommissioned'.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response
classification: vulnerability-response
topic_type: concept
last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 2
keywords: [managing retired configuration items, decommissioned configuration items, configure and import configuration items, creating configuration items, configuration items and their relationships, duplicate configuration items, modify a configuration item, configuration item, build a configuration item, retired configuration items, retired configuration item, configuration item status]
breadcrumb: [Automatic closing of vulnerable items and detections, Triage vulnerabilities automatically, Vulnerability Response remediation overview, Vulnerability Response, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Working with retired configuration items

Decommissioned configuration items \(CIs\) are moved to retired, archived, or deleted state in the Configuration Management Database \(CMDB\). Vulnerability Response contains vulnerable items \(VIs\) that are made up of CIs. When the state of a CI is updated to retired, the associated VIs are closed with the substate 'CI decommissioned'.

**Note:** Starting with v25.0.3 of Vulnerability Response, a new auto-close rule 'CI Decommissioned' is introduced to close the vulnerable items and detections related to decommissioned CIs.

If the CMDB changes the life cycle stage status of a CI to retired, you can choose to automatically close the associated VIs. For more information on automatically closing the associated VIs, see [Automatically close vulnerable items related to retired CIs](../task/auto-close-vis.md).

The retired CI is eventually archived or permanently deleted from the CMDB.

Impact of retiring the CIs:

-   **Impact on discovered items:** When the CI is retired, the related discovered item \(DI\) is marked as 'CI decommissioned'.
-   **Impact on vulnerable items:** When the state of a DI is updated to 'CI decommissioned', the associated VI is closed and the Notes section is automatically updated.

    **Note:** The state of a DI is automatically updated to 'CI decommissioned' whenever the life cycle state of a CI is updated. On the other hand, the state of a VI is updated to Closed only when the **Auto-close VIs linked to retired CIs** option is enabled. For more information on automatically closing the associated VIs, see [Automatically close vulnerable items related to retired CIs](../task/auto-close-vis.md).


The scheduled job **Close detections/VIs for decommissioned CIs** is run daily for the updated DIs. If the auto-close configuration option is enabled for retired CIs, it identifies the DI containing a decommissioned CI, and closes the related VI with the substate 'CI decommissioned'. If all the VIs in a remediation task are closed and their substate is CI decommissioned, the remediation task is also closed with the substate 'Canceled'. To view the decommissioned VIs, navigate to **Vulnerability Response** &gt; **Vulnerable Items** &gt; **Decommissioned**.

**Note:** If a CI moves back to the original state from the 'Retired' state, the 'Discovered Items' entry is updated as well. VIs are automatically opened when data is ingested again from the scanner.


```


---

```text
# Add affected CIs to the assessment record

Manually add the CIs in your organization that you think should be associated with the vulnerability assessment record but do not display in the affected CIs after initial assessment.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_analyst.vul\_event\_manager

## About this task

Add CIs that you know may have been impacted but do not show up after the assessment is done. You can only add CIs when the assessment record is in **Declared** or **Pending assessment** state.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **Workspaces** &gt; **Vulnerability Assessment Workspace**.

2.  Select the required assessment record from the list.

3.  Select **Add CIs**.

4.  Select the configuration items from the Configuration Items dialog box.

5.  Select **Add CIs**.

    The Vulnerability Response- Add Configuraton Items to Event background job initiates and a relevant notification displays.

6.  Select **View Status** on the notification to view the status of the running job.


## Result

Records are added to the Affected Configuration Items table with **Source** as *Manual*.

## What to do next

Navigate to the **Assessment** tab, and refresh the **Configurations Items \(Host/Infra\)** widget to view the CIs that you added.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response-workspaces\assign-priority-exposure-level-vr-va-ws.md

```text
---

locale: en-US
release: australia

classification: vulnerability-response-workspaces

last_updated: "2026-03-12"
reading_time_minutes: 1

breadcrumb: [Perform an assessment, Exploring vulnerability assessment, Explore the Vulnerability Assessment Workspace, Vulnerability Response Workspaces, Unified Security Exposure Management, Security Operations]
---

# Assign a priority and exposure level to the vulnerability assessment record

Manually assign priority and exposure level to the vulnerability assessment record.

## Before you begin

Role required: sn\_vul\_analyst.vul\_event\_manager

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **Workspaces** &gt; **Vulnerability Assessment workspace**.

2.  Select a vulnerability assessment record.

3.  Navigate to **Risk Attributes** in the **Details** tab.

4.  Select a **Priority** from the list of options.

5.  Select an **Exposure Level** from the list of options.

6.  Select **Save**.


## Result

When you update the priority of a vulnerability assessment \(VA\), the system also updates the priority of all associated vulnerable items \(VITs\) and application vulnerable items \(AVITs\).

If a VIT or AVIT is linked to multiple VA records, the system assigns the highest severity \(lowest priority number\) from all linked assessments.

## Example

If a VIT is associated with three VA records with priorities 2, 3, and 4, the system sets the VIT priority to 2 \(High\).

**Note:** This priority roll-down applies only to VITs and AVITs created through SAM NVD assessments or the Vulnerability Assessment Workspace \(Source = ServiceNow VR\). Vulnerable items imported from third-party scanners retain the priority determined by the scanner integration’s severity mapping.


```


### File: security-management\vulnerability-response-workspaces\create-vuln-assessment.md

```text
---
title: Create a vulnerability assessment record
description: Create assessment records for vulnerabilities of interest. After you create the assessment, you can initiate initial risk assessment, manually update the record, and calculate the risk score automatically.
locale: en-US
release: australia
product: Vulnerability Response Workspaces

last_updated: "2026-03-12"

---

# Create a vulnerability assessment record

Create assessment records for vulnerabilities of interest. After you create the assessment, you can initiate initial risk assessment, manually update the record, and calculate the risk score automatically.

## Before you begin

You need to be part of the base system Vulnerability Assessment Event Manager assignment group to be able to perform the tasks associated with vulnerability assessment. Role required: sn\_vul\_analyst.vul\_event\_manager

## About this task

You can create a vulnerability assessment record for vulnerabilities of interest either by associating the event record to a primary CVE or to an affected product.

## Procedure

1.  Navigate to **All** &gt; **Workspaces** &gt; **Vulnerability Assessment Workspace**.

2.  Select the **Vulnerability Assessment** icon.

3.  Select **New**.

4.  Enter an appropriate title in the **Title** field.

5.  Select a primary CVE to associate with the new assessment record from the **Primary CVE** field.

    **Note:** All the preliminary details on the assessment record are populated based on the product that you select or create or the primary CVE that you associate with the record.

6.  Set a CI filter to narrow the configuration items collected during assessment.

    **Note:**

    -   When the assessment runs, only CIs that match the filter conditions are collected. If no CI filter is set, all CIs related to the vulnerability or affected products are collected.
    -   You can also add or modify the CI filter after the record is created. For more information, see [Modify the vulnerability assessment record](modify-vuln-assessment-record.md#).
7.  Select **Submit**.

    The vulnerability assessment record with the title you had entered is created and displays in a new vulnerability assessment tab. By default, the vulnerability assessment record displays in the **Details** tab.

    **Note:** By default, the details of the primary CVE associated/ mapped are displayed.

    -   If no primary CVE is selected during the vulnerability assessment event record creation, a default record is created.
    -   If the primary CVE has associated CVEs then the related CVEs are populated in the **Vulnerability Entries** tab. You can add more associated CVEs from the **Vulnerability Entries** tab.
8.  Navigate to the **Affected Products** tab.

    1.  Select **New**.

    2.  On the form, fill in the fields.

<table id="table_ixb_mxc_cyb"><thead><tr><th>

Field

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>

Publisher

</td><td>

Publisher of the software.This is a mandatory field.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Product

</td><td>

Product name of the software.This is a mandatory field.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Version

</td><td>

Version of the software product.

</td></tr><tr><td>

Edition

</td><td>

Edition of the software.

</td></tr></tbody>
</table>        **Note:** If you try to add vulnerability for software that exists in the table with the status 'false', a duplicate record is not created. The status of the existing software is updated to 'true'.

    3.  Select **OK**.

9.  Select **Save**.



```
